verses.hebrew_full: וְלִבְנֵ֣י יִשָּׂשכָ֗ר תּוֹלָ֧ע וּפוּאָ֛ה (ישיב) יָשׁ֥וּב וְשִׁמְר֖וֹן אַרְבָּעָֽה
verses.translation: The sons of Issachar: Tola, Puah, Jashub, and Shimron—four.
verses.rashi: And of the sons of Issachar: Tola, and Puah, and Jashub, and Shimron In the Pentateuch (Gen. 46:13) it is written: “and Job.” Job was his name [originally], but since they settled themselves (נִתְיַשְּׁבוּ) to learn Torah, as it is written (below 12:33): “And of the sons of Issachar, who possessed understanding of the times,” he merited and was called Jashub (יָשוּב).

verses.hebrew_full: וּבְנֵ֣י תוֹלָ֗ע עֻזִּ֡י וּרְפָיָ֡ה וִ֠ירִיאֵ֠ל וְיַחְמַ֨י וְיִבְשָׂ֜ם וּשְׁמוּאֵ֗ל רָאשִׁ֤ים לְבֵית־אֲבוֹתָם֙ לְתוֹלָ֔ע גִּבּ֥וֹרֵי חַ֖יִל לְתֹלְדוֹתָ֑ם מִסְפָּרָם֙ בִּימֵ֣י דָוִ֔יד עֶשְׂרִֽים־וּשְׁנַ֥יִם אֶ֖לֶף וְשֵׁ֥שׁ מֵאֽוֹת
verses.translation: The sons of Tola: Uzzi, Rephaiah, Jeriel, Jahmai, Ibsam, Shemuel, chiefs of their clans, men of substance according to their lines; their number in the days of David was 22,600.
verses.rashi: And the sons of Tola, etc. “Their number in David’s time was twenty-two thousand and six hundred.” All these were the sons of Tola, aside from the sons of Uzzi.

verses.hebrew_full: וּבְנֵ֥י עֻזִּ֖י יִֽזְרַֽחְיָ֑ה וּבְנֵ֣י יִֽזְרַֽחְיָ֗ה מִיכָאֵ֡ל וְ֠עֹבַדְיָ֠ה וְיוֹאֵ֧ל יִשִּׁיָּ֛ה חֲמִשָּׁ֖ה רָאשִׁ֥ים כֻּלָּֽם
verses.translation: The sons of Uzzi: Izrahiah. And the sons of Izrahiah: Michael, Obadiah, Joel, and Isshiah—five. All of them were chiefs.
verses.rashi: And the sons of Uzzi, etc. “thirty-six thousand for they had many wives and sons.” And all these were aside from those twenty-two thousand and six hundred of the sons of Tola.

words.eng: Ishiah or Ishijah or Jesiah = "Jehovah will lend"

verses.hebrew_full: וַעֲלֵיהֶ֨ם לְתֹלְדוֹתָ֜ם לְבֵ֣ית אֲבוֹתָ֗ם גְּדוּדֵי֙ צְבָ֣א מִלְחָמָ֔ה שְׁלֹשִׁ֥ים וְשִׁשָּׁ֖ה אָ֑לֶף כִּי־הִרְבּ֥וּ נָשִׁ֖ים וּבָנִֽים
verses.translation: And together with them, by their lines, according to their clans, were units of the fighting force, 36,000, for they had many wives and sons.

verses.hebrew_full: וַאֲחֵיהֶ֗ם לְכֹל֙ מִשְׁפְּח֣וֹת יִשָּׂשכָ֔ר גִּבּוֹרֵ֖י חֲיָלִ֑ים שְׁמוֹנִ֤ים וְשִׁבְעָה֙ אֶ֔לֶף הִתְיַחְשָׂ֖ם לַכֹּֽל
verses.translation: Their kindred belonging to all the families of Issachar were in all 87,000 men of substance; they were all registered by genealogy.
verses.rashi: And their brethren of all the families both of the sons of Tola, both of the sons of Uzzi, both all David’s mighty warriors were eighty-seven thousand. all having genealogical records This means: according to all their genealogical records.

verses.hebrew_full: בִּנְיָמִ֗ן בֶּ֧לַע וָבֶ֛כֶר וִידִֽיעֲאֵ֖ל שְׁלֹשָֽׁה
verses.translation: [The sons of] Benjamin: Bela, Becher, and Jediael—three.
verses.rashi: Benjamin: Bela, and Becher, and Jediael This is the one called Ashbel in the “Book of the Just.” (Gen. 46:21).

verses.hebrew_full: וּבְנֵ֣י בֶ֗לַע אֶצְבּ֡וֹן וְעֻזִּ֡י וְ֠עֻזִּיאֵ֠ל וִירִימ֨וֹת וְעִירִ֜י חֲמִשָּׁ֗ה רָאשֵׁי֙ בֵּ֣ית אָב֔וֹת גִּבּוֹרֵ֖י חֲיָלִ֑ים וְהִתְיַחְשָׂ֗ם עֶשְׂרִ֤ים וּשְׁנַ֙יִם֙ אֶ֔לֶף וּשְׁלֹשִׁ֖ים וְאַרְבָּעָֽה
verses.translation: The sons of Bela: Ezbon, Uzzi, Uzziel, Jerimoth, and Iri—five, chiefs of clans, men of substance, registered by genealogy—22,034.

words.eng: Ezbon = "hasting to discern: I will be enlargement"
Jerimoth or Jeremoth = "He is Most High"

verses.hebrew_full: וּבְנֵ֣י בֶ֗כֶר זְמִירָ֡ה וְיוֹעָ֡שׁ וֶֽ֠אֱלִיעֶ֠זֶר וְאֶלְיוֹעֵינַ֤י וְעׇמְרִי֙ וִירֵמ֣וֹת וַאֲבִיָּ֔ה וַעֲנָת֖וֹת וְעָלָ֑מֶת כׇּל־אֵ֖לֶּה בְּנֵי־בָֽכֶר
verses.translation: The sons of Becher: Zemirah, Joash, Eliezer, Elioenai, Omri, Jeremoth, Abijah, Anathoth, and Alemeth. All these were the sons of Becher;

words.eng: Elioenai or Elihoenai = "unto Jehovah are my eyes"
Jerimoth or Jeremoth = "He is Most High"

verses.hebrew_full: וְהִתְיַחְשָׂ֣ם לְתֹלְדוֹתָ֗ם רָאשֵׁי֙ בֵּ֣ית אֲבוֹתָ֔ם גִּבּוֹרֵ֖י חָ֑יִל עֶשְׂרִ֥ים אֶ֖לֶף וּמָאתָֽיִם
verses.translation: and they were registered by genealogy according to their lines, as chiefs of their clans, men of substance—20,200.

verses.hebrew_full: וּבְנֵ֥י יְדִיעֲאֵ֖ל בִּלְהָ֑ן וּבְנֵ֣י בִלְהָ֗ן (יעיש) יְע֡וּשׁ וּ֠בִנְיָמִ֠ן וְאֵה֤וּד וּֽכְנַעֲנָה֙ וְזֵיתָ֔ן וְתַרְשִׁ֖ישׁ וַאֲחִישָֽׁחַר
verses.translation: The sons of Jediael: Bilhan. And the sons of Bilhan: Jeush, Benjamin, Ehud, Chenaanah, Zethan, Tarshish, and Ahishahar.

words.eng: Jehush or Jeush = "He hurries to aid"
a precious stone or semi-precious gem
Ahishahar = "my brother is dawn" or "brother of the dawn"

verses.hebrew_full: כׇּל־אֵ֜לֶּה בְּנֵ֤י יְדִֽיעֲאֵל֙ לְרָאשֵׁ֣י הָאָב֔וֹת גִּבּוֹרֵ֖י חֲיָלִ֑ים שִׁבְעָֽה־עָשָׂ֥ר אֶ֙לֶף֙ וּמָאתַ֔יִם יֹצְאֵ֥י צָבָ֖א לַמִּלְחָמָֽה
verses.translation: All these were the sons of Jediael, chiefs of the clans, men of substance—17,200, who made up the fighting force.

verses.hebrew_full: וְשֻׁפִּ֤ם וְחֻפִּם֙ בְּנֵ֣י עִ֔יר חֻשִׁ֖ם בְּנֵ֥י אַחֵֽר
verses.translation: And Shuppim and Huppim were the sons of Ir; Hushim the sons of Aher.
verses.rashi: And Shuppim and Huppim, the sons of Ir, Hushim, the sons of Aher Ezra, who wrote this book of genealogy, did not know whether they were of the sons of Benjamin or not. He therefore listed them alone. He was also in doubt because it is written: “And Machir took a wife of Huppim.”

verses.hebrew_full: בְּנֵ֣י נַפְתָּלִ֗י יַחֲצִיאֵ֧ל וְגוּנִ֛י וְיֵ֥צֶר וְשַׁלּ֖וּם בְּנֵ֥י בִלְהָֽה
verses.translation: The sons of Naphtali: Jahziel, Guni, Jezer, and Shallum, the descendants of Bilhah.
verses.rashi: The sons of Naphtali: Jahziel, etc. And why was no more of his genealogy traced? This is the reason, as explained at the end of Megillah Yerushalmi0 (see Com. Dig. 8:29): Ezra found three books, and each one of them contained part of the genealogy; that which he found he did not write, and of the sons of Naphtali he did not find any more. For this reason, the entire genealogy is traced with omissions, because he skipped from one book to another and combined them. And that which he could not write in this Book, he wrote in the Book of Ezra. You should know [that this is true] because it is written close by (9:1): “And all Israel traced their genealogy, and behold they are written in the book of the kings of Israel, and Judah was exiled to Babylon because of its treachery.” The meaning is that if you wish to know the genealogy of the Ten Tribes, go to Halah and Habor, the Gozan River, and the cities of Media, because their annals were exiled with them, but as for Judah I found their records in Babylon, and that which I found, I wrote.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּנֵ֣י מְנַשֶּׁ֔ה אַשְׂרִיאֵ֖ל אֲשֶׁ֣ר יָלָ֑דָה פִּֽילַגְשׁוֹ֙ הָֽאֲרַמִּיָּ֔ה יָלְדָ֕ה אֶת־מָכִ֖יר אֲבִ֥י גִלְעָֽד
verses.translation: The sons of Manasseh: Asriel, whom his Aramean concubine bore; she bore Machir the father of Gilead.
verses.rashi: The sons of Manasseh: Asriel whom she bore The meaning is: whom his wife bore to him. A similar case is (Num. 26:59): “... whom she bore to Levi in Egypt.” But his Aramean concubine bore Machir.

words.eng: Ashriel or Asriel = "I shall be prince of God"
sign of the definite direct object·Machir = "sold"

verses.hebrew_full: וּמָכִ֞יר לָקַ֤ח אִשָּׁה֙ לְחֻפִּ֣ים וּלְשֻׁפִּ֔ים וְשֵׁ֤ם אֲחֹתוֹ֙ מַֽעֲכָ֔ה וְשֵׁ֥ם הַשֵּׁנִ֖י צְלׇפְחָ֑ד וַתִּהְיֶ֥נָה לִצְלׇפְחָ֖ד בָּנֽוֹת
verses.translation: And Machir took wives for Huppim and for Shuppim. The name of his sister was Maacah. And the name of the second was Zelophehad; and Zelophehad had daughters.

words.eng: Abel Beth Maachah = "meadow of the house of Maachah"

verses.hebrew_full: וַתֵּ֨לֶד מַעֲכָ֤ה אֵֽשֶׁת־מָכִיר֙ בֵּ֔ן וַתִּקְרָ֤א שְׁמוֹ֙ פֶּ֔רֶשׁ וְשֵׁ֥ם אָחִ֖יו שָׁ֑רֶשׁ וּבָנָ֖יו אוּלָ֥ם וָרָֽקֶם
verses.translation: And Maacah the wife of Machir bore a son, and she named him Peresh; and the name of his brother was Sheresh; and his sons were Ulam and Rekem.

words.eng: Abel Beth Maachah = "meadow of the house of Maachah"

verses.hebrew_full: וּבְנֵ֥י אוּלָ֖ם בְּדָ֑ן אֵ֚לֶּה בְּנֵ֣י גִלְעָ֔ד בֶּן־מָכִ֖יר בֶּן־מְנַשֶּֽׁה
verses.translation: The sons of Ulam: Bedan. These were the sons of Gilead son of Machir son of Manasseh.

verses.hebrew_full: וַאֲחֹת֖וֹ הַמֹּלֶ֑כֶת יָֽלְדָה֙ אֶת־אִ֣ישְׁה֔וֹד וְאֶת־אֲבִיעֶ֖זֶר וְאֶת־מַחְלָֽה
verses.translation: And his sister Hammolecheth bore Ishhod, Abiezer, and Mahlah.

words.eng: to be or become king or queen
sign of the definite direct object·Ishod = "man of majesty"
sign of the definite direct object·Abiezer = "my father is help"
sign of the definite direct object·to be or become weak

verses.hebrew_full: וַיִּהְי֖וּ בְּנֵ֣י שְׁמִידָ֑ע אַחְיָ֣ן וָשֶׁ֔כֶם וְלִקְחִ֖י וַאֲנִיעָֽם
verses.translation: The sons of Shemida were Ahian, Shechem, Likhi, and Aniam.

words.eng: Aniam = "I am the people" or "lament of people"

verses.hebrew_full: וּבְנֵ֥י אֶפְרַ֖יִם שׁוּתָ֑לַח וּבֶ֤רֶד בְּנוֹ֙ וְתַ֣חַת בְּנ֔וֹ וְאֶלְעָדָ֥ה בְנ֖וֹ וְתַ֥חַת בְּנֽוֹ
verses.translation: The sons of Ephraim: Shuthelah, his son Bered, his son Tahath, his son Eleadah, his son Tahath,

words.eng: Ephraim = "double ash-heap: I shall be doubly fruitful"

verses.hebrew_full: וְזָבָ֥ד בְּנ֛וֹ וְשׁוּתֶ֥לַח בְּנ֖וֹ וְעֵ֣זֶר וְאֶלְעָ֑ד וַהֲרָג֗וּם אַנְשֵׁי־גַת֙ הַנּוֹלָדִ֣ים בָּאָ֔רֶץ כִּ֣י יָֽרְד֔וּ לָקַ֖חַת אֶת־מִקְנֵיהֶֽם
verses.translation: his son Zabad, his son Shuthelah, also Ezer and Elead. The residents of Gath, born in the land, killed them because they had gone down to take their cattle.
verses.rashi: and the men of Gath, etc. slew them because they came down to take their cattle They were unfamiliar with the ways of the roads and the ways out of the land, and the men of Gath, the natives of the land, slew them because they were familiar with that land.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·cattle

verses.hebrew_full: וַיִּתְאַבֵּ֛ל אֶפְרַ֥יִם אֲבִיהֶ֖ם יָמִ֣ים רַבִּ֑ים וַיָּבֹ֥אוּ אֶחָ֖יו לְנַחֲמֽוֹ
verses.translation: And Ephraim their father mourned many days, and his brothers came to comfort him.

words.eng: Ephraim = "double ash-heap: I shall be doubly fruitful"

verses.hebrew_full: וַיָּבֹא֙ אֶל־אִשְׁתּ֔וֹ וַתַּ֖הַר וַתֵּ֣לֶד בֵּ֑ן וַיִּקְרָ֤א אֶת־שְׁמוֹ֙ בְּרִיעָ֔ה כִּ֥י בְרָעָ֖ה הָיְתָ֥ה בְּבֵיתֽוֹ
verses.translation: He cohabited with his wife, who conceived and bore a son; and she named him Beriah, because it occurred when there was misfortune. in his house.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·name

verses.hebrew_full: וּבִתּ֣וֹ שֶֽׁאֱרָ֔ה וַתִּ֧בֶן אֶת־בֵּית־חוֹר֛וֹן הַתַּחְתּ֖וֹן וְאֶת־הָעֶלְי֑וֹן וְאֵ֖ת אֻזֵּ֥ן שֶׁאֱרָֽה
verses.translation: His daughter was Sheerah, who built both Lower and Upper Beth-horon, and Uzzen-sheerah.
verses.rashi: And his daughter was Sheerah, and she built... Beth Horon Sheerah built it. and Uzzen Sheerah She named the city after herself.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·nm·Beth-Horon
sign of the definite direct object·adj
sign of the definite direct object

verses.hebrew_full: וְרֶ֣פַח בְּנ֗וֹ וְרֶ֧שֶׁף וְתֶ֛לַח בְּנ֖וֹ וְתַ֥חַן בְּנֽוֹ
verses.translation: His son Rephah, his son Resheph, his son Telah, his son Tahan,
verses.rashi: And Rephah was his son [i.e., the son] of Beriah. and Resheph and Telah the sons of Rephah. This is the way he counts the generations.

verses.hebrew_full: לַעְדָּ֥ן בְּנ֛וֹ עַמִּיה֥וּד בְּנ֖וֹ אֱלִישָׁמָ֥ע בְּנֽוֹ
verses.translation: his son Ladan, his son Ammihud, his son Elishama,

verses.hebrew_full: נ֥וֹן בְּנ֖וֹ יְהוֹשֻׁ֥עַ בְּנֽוֹ
verses.translation: his son Non, his son Joshua.

verses.hebrew_full: וַֽאֲחֻזָּתָם֙ וּמֹ֣שְׁבוֹתָ֔ם בֵּֽית־אֵ֖ל וּבְנֹתֶ֑יהָ וְלַמִּזְרָ֣ח נַעֲרָ֔ן וְלַֽמַּעֲרָ֗ב גֶּ֤זֶר וּבְנֹתֶ֙יהָ֙ וּשְׁכֶ֣ם וּבְנֹתֶ֔יהָ עַד־עַיָּ֖ה וּבְנֹתֶֽיהָ
verses.translation: Their possessions and settlements were Bethel and its dependencies, and on the east Naaran, and on the west Gezer and its dependencies, Shechem and its dependencies, and Aiah and its dependencies;

verses.hebrew_full: וְעַל־יְדֵ֣י בְנֵֽי־מְנַשֶּׁ֗ה בֵּית־שְׁאָ֤ן וּבְנֹתֶ֙יהָ֙ תַּעְנַ֣ךְ וּבְנֹתֶ֔יהָ מְגִדּ֥וֹ וּבְנוֹתֶ֖יהָ דּ֣וֹר וּבְנוֹתֶ֑יהָ בְּאֵ֙לֶּה֙ יָֽשְׁב֔וּ בְּנֵ֥י יוֹסֵ֖ף בֶּן־יִשְׂרָאֵֽל
verses.translation: also along the borders of the Manassites, Beth-shean and its dependencies, Taanach and its dependencies, Megiddo and its dependencies, Dor and its dependencies. In these dwelt the sons of Joseph son of Israel.
verses.rashi: And alongside the sons of Manasseh were: Beth Shean Heb. וְעַל יְדֵי, lit., on the hands of or on the place of. The meaning is: alongside the sons of Manasseh, like (Num. 2:17): “every one in his position עַל יָדוֹ according to their standards.”

words.eng: nm·(b. h.) pr. n. pl. Bethshean (Scythopolis)

verses.hebrew_full: בְּנֵ֣י אָשֵׁ֗ר יִמְנָ֧ה וְיִשְׁוָ֛ה וְיִשְׁוִ֥י וּבְרִיעָ֖ה וְשֶׂ֥רַח אֲחוֹתָֽם
verses.translation: The sons of Asher: Imnah, Ishvah, Ishvi, Beriah, and their sister Serah.

words.eng: Imna or Imnah or Jimnah = "right hand"
Ishui or Ishuai or Isui or Jesui = "he resembles me"

verses.hebrew_full: וּבְנֵ֣י בְרִיעָ֔ה חֶ֖בֶר וּמַלְכִּיאֵ֑ל ה֖וּא אֲבִ֥י (ברזות) בִרְזָֽיִת
verses.translation: The sons of Beriah: Heber and Malchiel, who was the father of Birzaith.
verses.rashi: the father of Birzaith the governor of that city. But in Genesis Rabbah (71:9)[it says]: “Rabbi Levi and Rabbi Simon [gave different interpretations of Birzaith]. Rabbi Levi says: It means that his daughters were beautiful and were married to [high] priests who were anointed with olive oil. Rabbi Simon says: [It means] that his daughters were beautiful and married to kings who were anointed with the anointment oil, and since they had plenty of oil, they [the daughters] would anoint themselves with it. Therefore, they were beautiful, for concerning Asher it is written (Deut. 33:24): ‘and dip his foot in oil,’ and Rabbi Hanina said: “The warm water and the oil with which my mother anointed me restored me to my youth.”

words.eng: Birzavith or Birzath = "in leanness: choice olive"

verses.hebrew_full: וְחֶ֙בֶר֙ הוֹלִ֣יד אֶת־יַפְלֵ֔ט וְאֶת־שׁוֹמֵ֖ר וְאֶת־חוֹתָ֑ם וְאֵ֖ת שׁוּעָ֥א אֲחוֹתָֽם
verses.translation: Heber begot Japhlet, Shomer, Hotham, and their sister, Shua.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·Japhlet = "he will deliver"
sign of the definite direct object·Shomer = "keeper"
sign of the definite direct object·seal
sign of the definite direct object

verses.hebrew_full: וּבְנֵ֣י יַפְלֵ֔ט פָּסַ֥ךְ וּבִמְהָ֖ל וְעַשְׁוָ֑ת אֵ֖לֶּה בְּנֵ֥י יַפְלֵֽט
verses.translation: The sons of Japhlet: Pasach, Bimhal, and Ashvath. These were the sons of Japhlet.

verses.hebrew_full: וּבְנֵ֖י שָׁ֑מֶר אֲחִ֥י (ורוהגה) וְרׇהְגָּ֖ה (יחבה) וְחֻבָּ֥ה וַאֲרָֽם
verses.translation: The sons of Shemer: Ahi, Rohgah, Hubbah, and Aram.

verses.hebrew_full: וּבֶן־הֵ֖לֶם אָחִ֑יו צוֹפַ֥ח וְיִמְנָ֖ע וְשֵׁ֥לֶשׁ וְעָמָֽל
verses.translation: The son[s] of Helem his brother: Zophah, Imna, Shelesh, and Amal.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּנֵ֖י צוֹפָ֑ח ס֧וּחַ וְחַרְנֶ֛פֶר וְשׁוּעָ֖ל וּבֵרִ֥י וְיִמְרָֽה
verses.translation: The sons of Zophah: Suah, Harnepher, Shual, Beri, Imrah,

verses.hebrew_full: בֶּ֣צֶר וָה֗וֹד וְשַׁמָּ֧א וְשִׁלְשָׁ֛ה וְיִתְרָ֖ן וּבְאֵרָֽא
verses.translation: Bezer, Hod, Shamma, Shilshah, Ithran, and Beera.

verses.hebrew_full: וּבְנֵ֖י יֶ֑תֶר יְפֻנֶּ֥ה וּפִסְפָּ֖ה וַאְרָֽא
verses.translation: The sons of Jether: Jephunneh, Pispa, and Ara.

verses.hebrew_full: וּבְנֵ֖י עֻלָּ֑א אָרַ֥ח וְחַנִּיאֵ֖ל וְרִצְיָֽא
verses.translation: The sons of Ulla: Arah, Hanniel, and Rizia.

verses.hebrew_full: כׇּל־אֵ֣לֶּה בְנֵֽי־אָ֠שֵׁ֠ר רָאשֵׁ֨י בֵית־הָאָב֤וֹת בְּרוּרִים֙ גִּבּוֹרֵ֣י חֲיָלִ֔ים רָאשֵׁ֖י הַנְּשִׂיאִ֑ים וְהִתְיַחְשָׂ֤ם בַּצָּבָא֙ בַּמִּלְחָמָ֔ה מִסְפָּרָ֣ם אֲנָשִׁ֔ים עֶשְׂרִ֥ים וְשִׁשָּׁ֖ה אָֽלֶף
verses.translation: All of these men of Asher, chiefs of the clans, select men, men of substance, heads of the chieftains. And they were registered by genealogy according to fighting force; these men numbered 26,000.

verses.hebrew_full: וּבִ֨נְיָמִ֔ן הוֹלִ֖יד אֶת־בֶּ֣לַע בְּכֹר֑וֹ אַשְׁבֵּל֙ הַשֵּׁנִ֔י וְאַחְרַ֖ח הַשְּׁלִישִֽׁי
verses.translation: Benjamin begot Bela his first-born, Ashbel the second, Aharah the third,
verses.rashi: And Benjamin begot Bela his firstborn He already traced his genealogy (above 7:6-11), but since he wished to trace his genealogy until Saul, he commences to trace it from Benjamin. We read other names here and the entire genealogy above, and whoever wishes to study with exactitude the majority of the Levitic cities and the open land around them - they are in Joshua, because Ezra found various books.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·a swallowing
Ashbel = "a man in God: a man of Baal: fire of Bel: I will make a path"

verses.hebrew_full: נוֹחָה֙ הָרְבִיעִ֔י וְרָפָ֖א הַחֲמִישִֽׁי
verses.translation: Nohah the fourth, and Rapha the fifth.

verses.hebrew_full: וַיִּהְי֥וּ בָנִ֖ים לְבָ֑לַע אַדָּ֥ר וְגֵרָ֖א וַאֲבִיהֽוּד
verses.translation: And Bela had sons: Addar, Gera, Abihud,

verses.hebrew_full: וַאֲבִישׁ֥וּעַ וְנַעֲמָ֖ן וַאֲחֽוֹחַ
verses.translation: Abishua, Naaman, Ahoah,

verses.hebrew_full: וְגֵרָ֥א וּשְׁפוּפָ֖ן וְחוּרָֽם
verses.translation: Gera, Shephuphan, and Huram.

verses.hebrew_full: וְאֵ֖לֶּה בְּנֵ֣י אֵח֑וּד אֵ֣לֶּה הֵ֞ם רָאשֵׁ֤י אָבוֹת֙ לְי֣וֹשְׁבֵי גֶ֔בַע וַיַּגְל֖וּם אֶל־מָנָֽחַת
verses.translation: These were the sons of Ehud—they were chiefs of clans of the inhabitants of Geba, and they were exiled to Manahath:
verses.rashi: And they exiled them to Manahath Who exiled them? He explains this in the [next] verse: And Naaman and Ahiah and Gera - he exiled them. Manahath This is the name of a province, as it is written (above 2:52): “who ruled over half the Menuhoth,” and it is written (ibid. verse 54): “and half the Manahtites.”

verses.hebrew_full: וְנַעֲמָ֧ן וַאֲחִיָּ֛ה וְגֵרָ֖א ה֣וּא הֶגְלָ֑ם וְהוֹלִ֥יד אֶת־עֻזָּ֖א וְאֶת־אֲחִיחֻֽד
verses.translation: Naaman, Ahijah, and Gera—he exiled them and begot Uzza and Ahihud.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·Uzza or Uzzah = "strength"
sign of the definite direct object·Ahihud = "brother of mystery"

verses.hebrew_full: וְשַׁחֲרַ֗יִם הוֹלִיד֙ בִּשְׂדֵ֣ה מוֹאָ֔ב מִן־שִׁלְח֖וֹ אֹתָ֑ם חוּשִׁ֥ים וְאֶֽת־בַּעֲרָ֖א נָשָֽׁיו
verses.translation: And Shaharaim had sons in the country of Moab after he had sent away Hushim and Baara his wives.
verses.rashi: And Shaharaim begot He was one of the exiles, and after they sent him out of exile, he begot these. Hushim and Baara were his wives. These were the names of his wives.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object
sign of the definite direct object·Baara = "brutish"

verses.hebrew_full: וַיּ֖וֹלֶד מִן־חֹ֣דֶשׁ אִשְׁתּ֑וֹ אֶת־יוֹבָב֙ וְאֶת־צִבְיָ֔א וְאֶת־מֵישָׁ֖א וְאֶת־מַלְכָּֽם
verses.translation: He had sons by Hodesh his wife: Jobab, Zibia, Mesha, Malcam,

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·Jobab = "a desert"
sign of the definite direct object·Zibia = "roe"
sign of the definite direct object·Mesha = "freedom"
sign of the definite direct object·king

verses.hebrew_full: וְאֶת־יְע֥וּץ וְאֶת־שָֽׂכְיָ֖ה וְאֶת־מִרְמָ֑ה אֵ֥לֶּה בָנָ֖יו רָאשֵׁ֥י אָבֽוֹת
verses.translation: Jeuz, Sachiah, and Mirmah. These were his sons, chiefs of clans.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·Jeuz = "counsellor"
sign of the definite direct object·Shachia = "announcement"
sign of the definite direct object·deceit

verses.hebrew_full: וּמֵחֻשִׁ֛ים הוֹלִ֥יד אֶת־אֲבִיט֖וּב וְאֶת־אֶלְפָּֽעַל
verses.translation: He also begot by Hushim: Abitub and Elpaal.
verses.rashi: And from Hushim he begot This is Hushim his wife, mentioned above, and because there was an interruption with Hodesh his wife.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object
sign of the definite direct object·Elpaal = "God is maker"

verses.hebrew_full: וּבְנֵ֣י אֶלְפַּ֔עַל עֵ֥בֶר וּמִשְׁעָ֖ם וָשָׁ֑מֶד ה֚וּא בָּנָ֣ה אֶת־אוֹנ֔וֹ וְאֶת־לֹ֖ד וּבְנֹתֶֽיהָ
verses.translation: The sons of Elpaal: Eber, Misham, and Shemed, who built Ono and Lod with its dependencies,

words.eng: to pass over or by or through
sign of the definite direct object·vigour
sign of the definite direct object·Lod = "travail"

verses.hebrew_full: וּבְרִעָ֣ה וָשֶׁ֔מַע הֵ֚מָּה רָאשֵׁ֣י הָאָב֔וֹת לְיוֹשְׁבֵ֖י אַיָּל֑וֹן הֵ֥מָּה הִבְרִ֖יחוּ אֶת־י֥וֹשְׁבֵי גַֽת
verses.translation: and Beriah and Shema—they were chiefs of clans of the inhabitants of Aijalon, who put to flight the inhabitants of Gath;

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·to dwell

verses.hebrew_full: וְאַחְי֥וֹ שָׁשָׁ֖ק וִירֵמֽוֹת
verses.translation: and Ahio, Shashak, and Jeremoth.
verses.rashi: And Ahio, Shashak All these were the sons of Elpaal. Another explanation, which is more correct, is that from “Ahio, Shashak” until “and Joha,” all these are the sons of Beriah, and from “Zebadiah” until “Jobab,” all these are the sons of Elpaal, and from “And Jakim,” until “and Shimrath,” all these are the sons of Shimei.

words.eng: Jerimoth or Jeremoth = "He is Most High"

verses.hebrew_full: וּזְבַדְיָ֥ה וַעֲרָ֖ד וָעָֽדֶר
verses.translation: Zebadiah, Arad, Eder,

verses.hebrew_full: וּמִיכָאֵ֧ל וְיִשְׁפָּ֛ה וְיוֹחָ֖א בְּנֵ֥י בְרִיעָֽה
verses.translation: Michael, Ishpah, and Joha were sons of Beriah.

verses.hebrew_full: וּזְבַדְיָ֥ה וּמְשֻׁלָּ֖ם וְחִזְקִ֥י וָחָֽבֶר
verses.translation: Zebadiah, Meshullam, Hizki, Heber,

words.eng: to be in a covenant of peace

verses.hebrew_full: וְיִשְׁמְרַ֧י וְיִזְלִיאָ֛ה וְיוֹבָ֖ב בְּנֵ֥י אֶלְפָּֽעַל
verses.translation: Ishmerai, Izliah, and Jobab were the sons of Elpaal.

verses.hebrew_full: וְיָקִ֥ים וְזִכְרִ֖י וְזַבְדִּֽי
verses.translation: Jakim, Zichri, Zabdi,

verses.hebrew_full: וֶאֱלִיעֵינַ֥י וְצִלְּתַ֖י וֶאֱלִיאֵֽל
verses.translation: Elienai, Zillethai, Eliel,

words.eng: Eliel = "my God is God" or "El is God"

verses.hebrew_full: וַעֲדָיָ֧ה וּבְרָאיָ֛ה וְשִׁמְרָ֖ת בְּנֵ֥י שִׁמְעִֽי
verses.translation: Adaiah, Beraiah, and Shimrath were the sons of Shimei.

verses.hebrew_full: וְיִשְׁפָּ֥ן וָעֵ֖בֶר וֶאֱלִיאֵֽל
verses.translation: Ishpan, Eber, Eliel,

words.eng: to pass over or by or through
Eliel = "my God is God" or "El is God"

verses.hebrew_full: וְעַבְדּ֥וֹן וְזִכְרִ֖י וְחָנָֽן
verses.translation: Abdon, Zichri, Hanan,

verses.hebrew_full: וַחֲנַנְיָ֥ה וְעֵילָ֖ם וְעַנְתֹתִיָּֽה
verses.translation: Hananiah, Elam, Anthothiah,

verses.hebrew_full: וְיִפְדְיָ֥ה (ופניאל) וּפְנוּאֵ֖ל בְּנֵ֥י שָׁשָֽׁק
verses.translation: Iphdeiah, and Penuel were the sons of Shashak.

verses.hebrew_full: וְשַׁמְשְׁרַ֥י וּשְׁחַרְיָ֖ה וַעֲתַלְיָֽה
verses.translation: Shamsherai, Shehariah, Athaliah,

verses.hebrew_full: וְיַעֲרֶשְׁיָ֧ה וְאֵלִיָּ֛ה וְזִכְרִ֖י בְּנֵ֥י יְרֹחָֽם
verses.translation: Jaareshiah, Elijah, and Zichri were the sons of Jeroham.

verses.hebrew_full: אֵ֣לֶּה רָאשֵׁ֥י אָב֛וֹת לְתֹלְדוֹתָ֖ם רָאשִׁ֑ים אֵ֖לֶּה יָשְׁב֥וּ בִירוּשָׁלִָֽם
verses.translation: These were the chiefs of the clans, according to their lines. These chiefs dwelt in Jerusalem.

verses.hebrew_full: וּבְגִבְע֥וֹן יָשְׁב֖וּ אֲבִ֣י גִבְע֑וֹן וְשֵׁ֥ם אִשְׁתּ֖וֹ מַעֲכָֽה
verses.translation: The father of Gibeon dwelt in Gibeon, and the name of his wife was Maacah.
verses.rashi: And in Gibeon dwelt the leader of Gibeon This section until (verse 38): “...all these were the sons of Azel,” is written twice in this Book. Also the section (9:2): “And the first settlers, who were in their inheritance in their cities,” and the section (9:10): “And of the priests: Jedaiah and Jehoirib” are likewise in the Book of Nehemiah, as it is written (11: 3): “And these are the heads of the province.” This is what is explained at the end of Megillath Yerushalmi. Ezra found scrolls: the scroll of מְעוֹנִים, the scroll of זַאַטוּטֵי and the scrolls of הָאַחִים, and they rejected the words of the one and accepted the words of the two. Likewise, they found many genealogical records. When they found three or five, they rejected the minority and accepted the majority. When they found an even number, e.g. “And in Gibeon dwelt the leader of Gibeon,” he had to write it twice because the order of their genealogy is not uniform, and likewise, [in] “the first settlers,” he found an even number, which differed one from the other. [Therefore], it was written twice: here and in his [Ezra’s] book. Now why did he not write both of them here, including those that are in Ezra: “And these are the heads of the province, etc.”? Because he traced the genealogy there until, “And these are the priests and the Levites,” and he traced the genealogy here until Jedaiah, who was after Ezra. Now what purpose would it have to explain and to trace the genealogy here in Chronicles, the generations and the genealogy after Ezra, except for those who were alive during the First Temple period?

words.eng: Abel Beth Maachah = "meadow of the house of Maachah"

verses.hebrew_full: וּבְנ֥וֹ הַבְּכ֖וֹר עַבְדּ֑וֹן וְצ֥וּר וְקִ֖ישׁ וּבַ֥עַל וְנָדָֽב
verses.translation: His first-born son: Abdon; then Zur, Kish, Baal, Nadab,

verses.hebrew_full: וּגְד֥וֹר וְאַחְי֖וֹ וָזָֽכֶר
verses.translation: Gedor, Ahio, Zecher.

verses.hebrew_full: וּמִקְל֖וֹת הוֹלִ֣יד אֶת־שִׁמְאָ֑ה וְאַף־הֵ֗מָּה נֶ֧גֶד אֲחֵיהֶ֛ם יָשְׁב֥וּ בִירוּשָׁלִַ֖ם עִם־אֲחֵיהֶֽם
verses.translation: Mikloth begot Shimeah. And they dwelt in Jerusalem opposite their kindred, with their kindred.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·Shimeah = "report"

verses.hebrew_full: וְנֵר֙ הוֹלִ֣יד אֶת־קִ֔ישׁ וְקִ֖ישׁ הוֹלִ֣יד אֶת־שָׁא֑וּל וְשָׁא֗וּל הוֹלִ֤יד אֶת־יְהֽוֹנָתָן֙ וְאֶת־מַלְכִּישׁ֔וּעַ וְאֶת־אֲבִינָדָ֖ב וְאֶת־אֶשְׁבָּֽעַל
verses.translation: Ner begot Kish, Kish begot Saul, Saul begot Jonathan, Malchi-shua, Abinadab, and Eshbaal;

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·Kish = "bent"
sign of the definite direct object·subst
sign of the definite direct object·Jonathan or Jehonathan = "Jehovah has given"
sign of the definite direct object
sign of the definite direct object·Abinadab = "my father is noble" or "my father is willing"
sign of the definite direct object·Eshbaal = "a man of Baal"

verses.hebrew_full: וּבֶן־יְהוֹנָתָ֖ן מְרִ֣יב בָּ֑עַל וּמְרִ֥יב בַּ֖עַל הוֹלִ֥יד אֶת־מִיכָֽה
verses.translation: and the son of Jonathan was Merib-baal; and Merib-baal begot Micah.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·Micah or Micaiah or Michah = "who is like God"

verses.hebrew_full: וּבְנֵ֖י מִיכָ֑ה פִּית֥וֹן וָמֶ֖לֶךְ וְתַאְרֵ֥עַ וְאָחָֽז
verses.translation: The sons of Micah: Pithon, Melech, Taarea, and Ahaz.

words.eng: Micah or Micaiah or Michah = "who is like God"

verses.hebrew_full: וְאָחָז֙ הוֹלִ֣יד אֶת־יְהוֹעַדָּ֔ה וִיהוֹעַדָּ֗ה הוֹלִ֛יד אֶת־עָלֶ֥מֶת וְאֶת־עַזְמָ֖וֶת וְאֶת־זִמְרִ֑י וְזִמְרִ֖י הוֹלִ֥יד אֶת־מוֹצָֽא
verses.translation: Ahaz begot Jehoaddah; and Jehoaddah begot Alemeth, Azmaveth, and Zimri; Zimri begot Moza.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·Jehoadah = "Jehovah has adorned"
sign of the definite direct object·Alameth or Alemeth = "covering"
sign of the definite direct object·Azmaveth = "strong unto death"
sign of the definite direct object·Zimri = "my music"
sign of the definite direct object·act or place of going out or forth

verses.hebrew_full: וּמוֹצָ֖א הוֹלִ֣יד אֶת־בִּנְעָ֑א רָפָ֥ה בְנ֛וֹ אֶלְעָשָׂ֥ה בְנ֖וֹ אָצֵ֥ל בְּנֽוֹ
verses.translation: Moza begot Binea; his son Raphah; his son Eleasah, his son Azel.

words.eng: act or place of going out or forth
sign of the definite direct object·Binea or Bineah = fountain

verses.hebrew_full: וּלְאָצֵל֮ שִׁשָּׁ֣ה בָנִים֒ וְאֵ֣לֶּה שְׁמוֹתָ֗ם עַזְרִיקָ֥ם בֹּ֙כְרוּ֙ וְיִשְׁמָעֵ֣אל וּשְׁעַרְיָ֔ה וְעֹבַדְיָ֖ה וְחָנָ֑ן כׇּל־אֵ֖לֶּה בְּנֵ֥י אָצַֽל
verses.translation: Azel had six sons, and these are their names: Azrikam, Bocheru, Ishmael, Sheariah, Obadiah, and Hanan. All these were the sons of Azel.

verses.hebrew_full: וּבְנֵ֖י עֵ֣שֶׁק אָחִ֑יו אוּלָ֣ם בְּכֹר֔וֹ יְעוּשׁ֙ הַשֵּׁנִ֔י וֶאֱלִיפֶ֖לֶט הַשְּׁלִשִֽׁי
verses.translation: The sons of Eshek his brother: Ulam his first-born, Jeush the second, and Eliphelet the third.

words.eng: Jehush or Jeush = "He hurries to aid"
Elpalet or Eliphalet or Eliphelet = "God is deliverance"

verses.hebrew_full: וַיִּהְי֣וּ בְנֵֽי־א֠וּלָ֠ם אֲנָשִׁ֨ים גִּבּוֹרֵי־חַ֜יִל דֹּ֣רְכֵי קֶ֗שֶׁת וּמַרְבִּ֤ים בָּנִים֙ וּבְנֵ֣י בָנִ֔ים מֵאָ֖ה וַחֲמִשִּׁ֑ים כׇּל־אֵ֖לֶּה מִבְּנֵ֥י בִנְיָמִֽן
verses.translation: The descendants of Ulam—men of substance, who drew the bow, had many children and grandchildren—one hundred and fifty; all these were Benjaminites.

verses.hebrew_full: וְכׇל־יִשְׂרָאֵל֙ הִתְיַחְשׂ֔וּ וְהִנָּ֣ם כְּתוּבִ֔ים עַל־סֵ֖פֶר מַלְכֵ֣י יִשְׂרָאֵ֑ל וִיהוּדָ֛ה הׇגְל֥וּ לְבָבֶ֖ל בְּמַעֲלָֽם
verses.translation: All Israel was registered by genealogies; and these are in the book of the kings of Israel. And Judah was taken into exile in Babylon because of their trespass.
verses.rashi: And all Israel traced their genealogy I related to you part of their genealogy, which I found, but the greater part of their genealogy is in the Book of the Kings of Israel, but the Judeans, who were exiled to Babylon because of their treachery, with whom I, Ezra, was - I found their genealogy written, and I recorded it.

verses.hebrew_full: וְהַיּֽוֹשְׁבִים֙ הָרִ֣אשֹׁנִ֔ים אֲשֶׁ֥ר בַּאֲחֻזָּתָ֖ם בְּעָרֵיהֶ֑ם יִשְׂרָאֵל֙ הַכֹּ֣הֲנִ֔ים הַלְוִיִּ֖ם וְהַנְּתִינִֽים
verses.translation: The first to settle in their towns, on their property, were Israelites, priests, Levites, and temple servants,

verses.hebrew_full: וּבִירוּשָׁלִַ֙ם֙ יָֽשְׁב֔וּ מִן־בְּנֵ֥י יְהוּדָ֖ה וּמִן־בְּנֵ֣י בִנְיָמִ֑ן וּמִן־בְּנֵ֥י אֶפְרַ֖יִם וּמְנַשֶּֽׁה
verses.translation: while some of the Judahites and some of the Benjaminites and some of the Ephraimites and Manassehites settled in Jerusalem;

words.eng: Ephraim = "double ash-heap: I shall be doubly fruitful"

verses.hebrew_full: עוּתַ֨י בֶּן־עַמִּיה֤וּד בֶּן־עׇמְרִי֙ בֶּן־אִמְרִ֣י בֶן־(בנימן)־בָּנִ֔י מִן־בְּנֵי־פֶ֖רֶץ בֶּן־יְהוּדָֽה
verses.translation: Uthai son of Ammihud son of Omri son of Imri son of Bani, from the sons of Perez son of Judah;

verses.hebrew_full: וּמִן־הַשִּׁ֣ילוֹנִ֔י עֲשָׂיָ֥ה הַבְּכ֖וֹר וּבָנָֽיו
verses.translation: and of the Shilonites: Asaiah the first-born and his sons.

verses.hebrew_full: וּמִן־בְּנֵי־זֶ֖רַח יְעוּאֵ֑ל וַאֲחֵיהֶ֖ם שֵׁשׁ־מֵא֥וֹת וְתִשְׁעִֽים
verses.translation: Of the sons of Zerah: Jeuel and their kindred—690.

verses.hebrew_full: וּמִן־בְּנֵ֖י בִּנְיָמִ֑ן סַלּוּא֙ בֶּן־מְשֻׁלָּ֔ם בֶּן־הוֹדַוְיָ֖ה בֶּן־הַסְּנֻאָֽה
verses.translation: Of the Benjaminites: Sallu son of Meshullam son of Hodaviah son of Hassenuah,

verses.hebrew_full: וְיִבְנְיָה֙ בֶּן־יְרֹחָ֔ם וְאֵלָ֥ה בֶן־עֻזִּ֖י בֶּן־מִכְרִ֑י וּמְשֻׁלָּם֙ בֶּן־שְׁפַטְיָ֔ה בֶּן־רְעוּאֵ֖ל בֶּן־יִבְנִיָּֽה
verses.translation: Ibneiah son of Jeroham, Elah son of Uzzi son of Michri, and Meshullam son of Shephatiah son of Reuel son of Ibneiah;

words.eng: to be in a covenant of peace

verses.hebrew_full: וַֽאֲחֵיהֶם֙ לְתֹ֣לְדוֹתָ֔ם תְּשַׁ֥ע מֵא֖וֹת וַחֲמִשִּׁ֣ים וְשִׁשָּׁ֑ה כׇּל־אֵ֣לֶּה אֲנָשִׁ֔ים רָאשֵׁ֥י אָב֖וֹת לְבֵ֥ית אֲבֹתֵיהֶֽם
verses.translation: and their kindred, according to their lines—956. All these were chiefs of their ancestral clans.

verses.hebrew_full: וּמִן־הַֽכֹּהֲנִ֑ים יְדַעְיָ֥ה וִיהוֹיָרִ֖יב וְיָכִֽין
verses.translation: Of the priests: Jedaiah, Jehoiarib, Jachin,

verses.hebrew_full: וַעֲזַרְיָ֨ה בֶן־חִלְקִיָּ֜ה בֶּן־מְשֻׁלָּ֣ם בֶּן־צָד֗וֹק בֶּן־מְרָיוֹת֙ בֶּן־אֲחִיט֔וּב נְגִ֖יד בֵּ֥ית הָאֱלֹהִֽים
verses.translation: and Azariah son of Hilkiah son of Meshullam son of Zadok son of Meraioth son of Ahitub, chief officer of the House of God;

verses.hebrew_full: וַעֲדָיָה֙ בֶּן־יְרֹחָ֔ם בֶּן־פַּשְׁח֖וּר בֶּן־מַלְכִּיָּ֑ה וּמַעְשַׂ֨י בֶּן־עֲדִיאֵ֧ל בֶּן־יַחְזֵ֛רָה בֶּן־מְשֻׁלָּ֥ם בֶּן־מְשִׁלֵּמִ֖ית בֶּן־אִמֵּֽר
verses.translation: and Adaiah son of Jeroham son of Pashhur son of Malchijah, and Maasai son of Adiel son of Jahzerah son of Meshullam son of Meshillemith son of Immer,

verses.hebrew_full: וַאֲחֵיהֶ֗ם רָאשִׁים֙ לְבֵ֣ית אֲבוֹתָ֔ם אֶ֕לֶף וּשְׁבַ֥ע מֵא֖וֹת וְשִׁשִּׁ֑ים גִּבּ֣וֹרֵי חֵ֔יל מְלֶ֖אכֶת עֲבוֹדַ֥ת בֵּית־הָאֱלֹהִֽים
verses.translation: together with their kindred, chiefs of their clans—1,760, men of substance for the work of the service of the House of God.

verses.hebrew_full: וּמִֽן־הַלְוִיִּ֑ם שְׁמַֽעְיָ֧ה בֶן־חַשּׁ֛וּב בֶּן־עַזְרִיקָ֥ם בֶּן־חֲשַׁבְיָ֖ה מִן־בְּנֵ֥י מְרָרִֽי
verses.translation: Of the Levites: Shemaiah son of Hasshub son of Azrikam son of Hashabiah, of the sons of Merari;

verses.hebrew_full: וּבַקְבַּקַּ֥ר חֶ֖רֶשׁ וְגָלָ֑ל וּמַתַּנְיָה֙ בֶּן־מִיכָ֔א בֶּן־זִכְרִ֖י בֶּן־אָסָֽף
verses.translation: and Bakbakkar, Heresh, Galal, and Mattaniah son of Mica son of Zichri son of Asaph;

verses.hebrew_full: וְעֹֽבַדְיָה֙ בֶּֽן־שְׁמַעְיָ֔ה בֶּן־גָּלָ֖ל בֶּן־יְדוּת֑וּן וּבֶרֶכְיָ֤ה בֶן־אָסָא֙ בֶּן־אֶלְקָנָ֔ה הַיּוֹשֵׁ֖ב בְּחַצְרֵ֥י נְטוֹפָתִֽי
verses.translation: and Obadiah son of Shemaiah son of Galal son of Jeduthun, and Berechiah son of Asa son of Elkanah, who dwelt in the villages of the Netophathites.

words.eng: between·Elkanah = "God has possessed" or "God has created"

verses.hebrew_full: וְהַשֹּֽׁעֲרִים֙ שַׁלּ֣וּם וְעַקּ֔וּב וְטַלְמֹ֖ן וַאֲחִימָ֑ן וַאֲחִיהֶ֥ם שַׁלּ֖וּם הָרֹֽאשׁ
verses.translation: The gatekeepers were: Shallum, Akkub, Talmon, Ahiman; and their kinsman Shallum was the chief

verses.hebrew_full: וְֽעַד־הֵ֔נָּה בְּשַׁ֥עַר הַמֶּ֖לֶךְ מִזְרָ֑חָה הֵ֚מָּה הַשֹּׁ֣עֲרִ֔ים לְמַחֲנ֖וֹת בְּנֵ֥י לֵוִֽי
verses.translation: hitherto in the King’s Gate on the east. They were the keepers belonging to the Levite camp.
verses.rashi: And from here until the king’s gate to the east The meaning of עַד is: from here until a gate named the King’s Gate to the east. they are the gate sentries Shallum and his brethren of the camps of the sons of Levi.

words.eng: ‘Levi’ — son of Jacob and Leah (see Gen. 29:34).

verses.hebrew_full: וְשַׁלּ֣וּם בֶּן־ק֠וֹרֵ֠א בֶּן־אֶבְיָסָ֨ף בֶּן־קֹ֜רַח וְֽאֶחָ֧יו לְבֵית־אָבִ֣יו הַקׇּרְחִ֗ים עַ֚ל מְלֶ֣אכֶת הָעֲבוֹדָ֔ה שֹׁמְרֵ֥י הַסִּפִּ֖ים לָאֹ֑הֶל וַאֲבֹֽתֵיהֶם֙ עַל־מַחֲנֵ֣ה יְהֹוָ֔ה שֹׁמְרֵ֖י הַמָּבֽוֹא
verses.translation: Shallum son of Kore son of Ebiasaph son of Korah, and his kindred of his clan, the Korahites, were in charge of the work of the service, guards of the threshold of the Tent; their ancestors had been guards of the entrance to GOD’s camp.
verses.rashi: the guards of the thresholds of the Tent Shallum the son of Kore and his brethren of his father’s house, the Korahites, were the guards of the thresholds of the Tent of Meeting and did not allow any person to enter the Tent of Meeting, except the priests in their service

verses.hebrew_full: וּפִֽינְחָ֣ס בֶּן־אֶלְעָזָ֗ר נָגִ֨יד הָיָ֧ה עֲלֵיהֶ֛ם לְפָנִ֖ים יְהֹוָ֥ה עִמּֽוֹ
verses.translation: And Phinehas son of Eleazar was the chief officer over them in time past; GOD was with him.
verses.rashi: And Phinehas the son of Eleazar was the ruler over them in time past, the Lord being with him [This means] that he prophesied in time past, and our Sages said that this is Phinehas the priest, and because he did not annul Jephthah’s vow, the Shechinah left him.

verses.hebrew_full: זְכַרְיָה֙ בֶּ֣ן מְשֶֽׁלֶמְיָ֔ה שֹׁעֵ֥ר פֶּ֖תַח לְאֹ֥הֶל מוֹעֵֽד
verses.translation: Zechariah the son of Meshelemiah was gatekeeper at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting.

verses.hebrew_full: כֻּלָּ֤ם הַבְּרוּרִים֙ לְשֹׁעֲרִ֣ים בַּסִּפִּ֔ים מָאתַ֖יִם וּשְׁנֵ֣ים עָשָׂ֑ר הֵ֤מָּה בְחַצְרֵיהֶם֙ הִתְיַחְשָׂ֔ם הֵ֣מָּה יִסַּ֥ד דָּוִ֛יד וּשְׁמוּאֵ֥ל הָרֹאֶ֖ה בֶּאֱמוּנָתָֽם
verses.translation: All these, who were selected as gatekeepers at the thresholds, were 212. They were selected by genealogies in their villages. David and Samuel the seer established them in their office of trust.
verses.rashi: whom David and Samuel the seer established permanently - Heb. בֶּאֱמוּנָתָם, in their permanence.

verses.hebrew_full: וְהֵ֨ם וּבְנֵיהֶ֜ם עַל־הַשְּׁעָרִ֧ים לְבֵית־יְהֹוָ֛ה לְבֵ֥ית הָאֹ֖הֶל לְמִשְׁמָרֽוֹת
verses.translation: They and their descendants were in charge of the gates of the House of GOD, that is, the House of the Tent, as guards.

verses.hebrew_full: לְאַרְבַּ֣ע רוּח֔וֹת יִהְי֖וּ הַשֹּׁעֲרִ֑ים מִזְרָ֥ח יָ֖מָּה צָפ֥וֹנָה וָנֶֽגְבָּה
verses.translation: The gatekeepers were on the four sides, east, west, north, and south;

verses.hebrew_full: וַאֲחֵיהֶ֨ם בְּחַצְרֵיהֶ֜ם לָב֨וֹא לְשִׁבְעַ֧ת הַיָּמִ֛ים מֵעֵ֥ת אֶל־עֵ֖ת עִם־אֵֽלֶּה
verses.translation: and their kindred in their villages were obliged to join them every seven days, according to a fixed schedule.
verses.rashi: for seven days at the end of the seven days. from time to time from seven to seven, each watch in its week.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֣י בֶאֱמוּנָ֞ה הֵ֗מָּה אַרְבַּ֙עַת֙ גִּבֹּרֵ֣י הַשֹּׁעֲרִ֔ים הֵ֖ם הַלְוִיִּ֑ם וְהָיוּ֙ עַל־הַלְּשָׁכ֔וֹת וְעַ֥ל הָאֹצְר֖וֹת בֵּ֥ית הָאֱלֹהִֽים
verses.translation: The four chief gatekeepers, who were Levites, were entrusted to be over the chambers and the treasuries of the House of God.

verses.hebrew_full: וּסְבִיב֥וֹת בֵּית־הָאֱלֹהִ֖ים יָלִ֑ינוּ כִּֽי־עֲלֵיהֶ֣ם מִשְׁמֶ֔רֶת וְהֵ֥ם עַל־הַמַּפְתֵּ֖חַ וְלַבֹּ֥קֶר לַבֹּֽקֶר
verses.translation: They spent the night near the House of God; for they had to do guard duty, and they were in charge of opening it every morning.
verses.rashi: and they were [appointed] over the key[s] over the key[s] of the forecourt. and every morning to open.

verses.hebrew_full: וּמֵהֶ֖ם עַל־כְּלֵ֣י הָעֲבוֹדָ֑ה כִּֽי־בְמִסְפָּ֣ר יְבִיא֔וּם וּבְמִסְפָּ֖ר יוֹצִיאֽוּם
verses.translation: Some of them had charge of the service vessels, for they were counted when they were brought back and taken out.
verses.rashi: And some of them were in charge of the service vessels כְּלֵי הָעֲבוֹדָה, the service vessels - they would take them out by number and bring them by number.

verses.hebrew_full: וּמֵהֶ֗ם מְמֻנִּים֙ עַל־הַכֵּלִ֔ים וְעַ֖ל כׇּל־כְּלֵ֣י הַקֹּ֑דֶשׁ וְעַל־הַסֹּ֙לֶת֙ וְהַיַּ֣יִן וְהַשֶּׁ֔מֶן וְהַלְּבוֹנָ֖ה וְהַבְּשָׂמִֽים
verses.translation: Some of them were in charge of the vessels and all the holy vessels, and of the flour, wine, oil, incense, and spices.

verses.hebrew_full: וּמִן־בְּנֵי֙ הַכֹּ֣הֲנִ֔ים רֹקְחֵ֥י הַמִּרְקַ֖חַת לַבְּשָׂמִֽים
verses.translation: Some of the priests blended the compound of spices.

verses.hebrew_full: וּמַתִּתְיָה֙ מִן־הַלְוִיִּ֔ם ה֥וּא הַבְּכ֖וֹר לְשַׁלֻּ֣ם הַקׇּרְחִ֑י בֶּאֱמוּנָ֕ה עַ֖ל מַעֲשֵׂ֥ה הַחֲבִתִּֽים
verses.translation: Mattithiah, one of the Levites, the first-born of Shallum the Korahite, was entrusted with making the flat cakes.

verses.hebrew_full: וּמִן־בְּנֵ֧י הַקְּהָתִ֛י מִן־אֲחֵיהֶ֖ם עַל־לֶ֣חֶם הַֽמַּעֲרָ֑כֶת לְהָכִ֖ין שַׁבַּ֥ת שַׁבָּֽת
verses.translation: Also some of their Kohathite kindred had charge of the rows of bread, to prepare them for each sabbath.

verses.hebrew_full: וְאֵ֣לֶּה הַ֠מְשֹׁרְרִ֠ים רָאשֵׁ֨י אָב֧וֹת לַלְוִיִּ֛ם בַּלְּשָׁכֹ֖ת (פטירים) פְּטוּרִ֑ים כִּֽי־יוֹמָ֥ם וָלַ֛יְלָה עֲלֵיהֶ֖ם בַּמְּלָאכָֽה
verses.translation: Now these are the singers, the chiefs of Levitical clans who remained in the chambers free of other service, for they were on duty day and night.
verses.rashi: And these are the singers, etc. who were exempt from any manner of work, except to sing a song

verses.hebrew_full: אֵ֩לֶּה֩ רָאשֵׁ֨י הָאָב֧וֹת לַלְוִיִּ֛ם לְתֹלְדוֹתָ֖ם רָאשִׁ֑ים אֵ֖לֶּה יָשְׁב֥וּ בִירוּשָׁלִָֽם
verses.translation: These were chiefs of Levitical clans, according to their lines; these chiefs lived in Jerusalem.

verses.hebrew_full: וּבְגִבְע֛וֹן יָשְׁב֥וּ אֲבִי־גִבְע֖וֹן (יעואל) יְעִיאֵ֑ל וְשֵׁ֥ם אִשְׁתּ֖וֹ מַעֲכָֽה
verses.translation: The father of Gibeon, Jeiel, lived in Gibeon, and the name of his wife was Maacah.

words.eng: Abel Beth Maachah = "meadow of the house of Maachah"

verses.hebrew_full: וּבְנ֥וֹ הַבְּכ֖וֹר עַבְדּ֑וֹן וְצ֣וּר וְקִ֔ישׁ וּבַ֥עַל וְנֵ֖ר וְנָדָֽב
verses.translation: His first-born son, Abdon; then Zur, Kish, Baal, Ner, Nadab,

verses.hebrew_full: וּגְד֣וֹר וְאַחְי֔וֹ וּזְכַרְיָ֖ה וּמִקְלֽוֹת
verses.translation: Gedor, Ahio, Zechariah, and Mikloth;

verses.hebrew_full: וּמִקְל֖וֹת הוֹלִ֣יד אֶת־שִׁמְאָ֑ם וְאַף־הֵ֗ם נֶ֧גֶד אֲחֵיהֶ֛ם יָשְׁב֥וּ בִירוּשָׁלִַ֖ם עִם־אֲחֵיהֶֽם
verses.translation: Mikloth begot Shimeam; and they lived in Jerusalem opposite their kindred, with their kindred.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·Shimeam = "their fame"

verses.hebrew_full: וְנֵר֙ הוֹלִ֣יד אֶת־קִ֔ישׁ וְקִ֖ישׁ הוֹלִ֣יד אֶת־שָׁא֑וּל וְשָׁא֗וּל הוֹלִ֤יד אֶת־יְהֽוֹנָתָן֙ וְאֶת־מַלְכִּישׁ֔וּעַ וְאֶת־אֲבִינָדָ֖ב וְאֶת־אֶשְׁבָּֽעַל
verses.translation: Ner begot Kish, Kish begot Saul, Saul begot Jonathan, Malchi-shua, Abinadab, and Eshbaal;
verses.rashi: Eshbaal That is Ish Bosheth, and he is nicknamed “bosheth,” because “baal” is an expression of lordship, and he nicknamed him “bosheth,” an expression of disgrace.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·Kish = "bent"
sign of the definite direct object·subst
sign of the definite direct object·Jonathan or Jehonathan = "Jehovah has given"
sign of the definite direct object
sign of the definite direct object·Abinadab = "my father is noble" or "my father is willing"
sign of the definite direct object·Eshbaal = "a man of Baal"

verses.hebrew_full: וּבֶן־יְהוֹנָתָ֖ן מְרִ֣יב בָּ֑עַל וּמְרִי־בַ֖עַל הוֹלִ֥יד אֶת־מִיכָֽה
verses.translation: and the son of Jonathan was Merib-baal; and Merib-baal begot Micah.
verses.rashi: Merib-Baal That is Mephibosheth (II Sam 4:4). Said Rabbi Isaac the son of Samuel of Narbonne: From the time that Israel worshipped the Baal, the Baal was called by the name of Bosheth (shame), as it is written (Jer. 11:13): “altars for the shameful thing, etc.,” for the Baal is a shameful thing. Gideon was called Jerubaal, based on the expression (Jud. 6:32): “Let Baal contend with him.” He was also called Jerubesheth, for it is written concerning Abimelech: “who smote Abimelech the son of Jerubesheth, etc.” in the Book of Samuel (II 11: 21). He is called Jerubesheth and Jerubaal, because Baal and Bosheth are synonymous.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·Micah or Micaiah or Michah = "who is like God"

verses.hebrew_full: וּבְנֵ֖י מִיכָ֑ה פִּיתֹ֥ן וָמֶ֖לֶךְ וְתַחְרֵֽעַ
verses.translation: The sons of Micah: Pithon, Melech, Taharea;

words.eng: Micah or Micaiah or Michah = "who is like God"

verses.hebrew_full: וְאָחָז֙ הוֹלִ֣יד אֶת־יַעְרָ֔ה וְיַעְרָ֗ה הוֹלִ֛יד אֶת־עָלֶ֥מֶת וְאֶת־עַזְמָ֖וֶת וְאֶת־זִמְרִ֑י וְזִמְרִ֖י הוֹלִ֥יד אֶת־מוֹצָֽא
verses.translation: Ahaz begot Jarah, and Jarah begot Alemeth, Azmaveth, and Zimri; Zimri begot Moza.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·to be bare
sign of the definite direct object·Alameth or Alemeth = "covering"
sign of the definite direct object·Azmaveth = "strong unto death"
sign of the definite direct object·Zimri = "my music"
sign of the definite direct object·act or place of going out or forth

verses.hebrew_full: וּמוֹצָ֖א הוֹלִ֣יד אֶת־בִּנְעָ֑א וּרְפָיָ֥ה בְנ֛וֹ אֶלְעָשָׂ֥ה בְנ֖וֹ אָצֵ֥ל בְּנֽוֹ
verses.translation: Moza begot Binea; his son was Rephaiah, his son Eleasah, his son Azel.

words.eng: act or place of going out or forth
sign of the definite direct object·Binea or Bineah = fountain

verses.hebrew_full: וּלְאָצֵל֮ שִׁשָּׁ֣ה בָנִים֒ וְאֵ֣לֶּה שְׁמוֹתָ֗ם עַזְרִיקָ֥ם בֹּ֙כְרוּ֙ וְיִשְׁמָעֵ֣אל וּשְׁעַרְיָ֔ה וְעֹבַדְיָ֖ה וְחָנָ֑ן אֵ֖לֶּה בְּנֵ֥י אָצַֽל
verses.translation: Azel had six sons and these were their names: Azrikam, Bocheru, Ishmael, Sheariah, Obadiah, and Hanan. These were the sons of Azel.

verses.hebrew_full: וּפְלִשְׁתִּ֖ים נִלְחֲמ֣וּ בְיִשְׂרָאֵ֑ל וַיָּ֤נׇס אִֽישׁ־יִשְׂרָאֵל֙ מִפְּנֵ֣י פְלִשְׁתִּ֔ים וַיִּפְּל֥וּ חֲלָלִ֖ים בְּהַ֥ר גִּלְבֹּֽעַ
verses.translation: The Philistines attacked Israel, and Israel’s side fled before the Philistines—and [many] fell on Mount Gilboa.
verses.rashi: And the Philistines fought with Israel He relates only Saul’s downfall; when relating the story of David, he does not tell of his disgrace, only of his heroism and greatness, because the Book pertains to him and to the kings of Judah.

verses.hebrew_full: וַיַּדְבְּק֣וּ פְלִשְׁתִּ֔ים אַחֲרֵ֥י שָׁא֖וּל וְאַחֲרֵ֣י בָנָ֑יו וַיַּכּ֣וּ פְלִשְׁתִּ֗ים אֶת־יוֹנָתָ֧ן וְאֶת־אֲבִינָדָ֛ב וְאֶת־מַלְכִּישׁ֖וּעַ בְּנֵ֥י שָׁאֽוּל
verses.translation: The Philistines pursued Saul and his sons, and the Philistines struck down Jonathan, Abinadab, and Malchi-shua, sons of Saul.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·Jonathan or Jehonathan = "Jehovah has given"
sign of the definite direct object·Abinadab = "my father is noble" or "my father is willing"
sign of the definite direct object

verses.hebrew_full: וַתִּכְבַּ֤ד הַמִּלְחָמָה֙ עַל־שָׁא֔וּל וַיִּמְצָאֻ֖הוּ הַמּוֹרִ֣ים בַּקָּ֑שֶׁת וַיָּ֖חֶל מִן־הַיּוֹרִֽים
verses.translation: The battle raged around Saul, and the archers hit him, and he was wounded by the archers.
verses.rashi: and the archers discovered him Come and see that (Prov. 21:30): “... there is neither wisdom nor understanding, etc. against the Lord,” because man’s ways are before the Lord. Benjamin’s enemies would fall before them [when they used] the bow because they were well trained with the bow, as it is written: “[Ehud the son of Gera, the Benjamite,] a man with a shriveled right hand,” (Judges 3:15). And so it is written, concerning Jonathan (I Samuel 20:20): “And I shall shoot three arrows to the side, as though I shot at a mark.” And it is written below (I Chron. 12:2) concerning Benjamin: “Armed with bows, shooting with the right and left hands, etc.” and concerning Asa in this Book (II 14:7): “... and out of Benjamin, who bore shields and drew bows, etc.” But when the Lord turned away from Saul [who was from the tribe of Benjamin], he fell into the hands of the archers, into something that was his own craft. and he was terrified of the archers וַיָחֶל like (Esther 4:4): “...and the queen was extremely terrified (וַתִּתְחַלְחַל).”

verses.hebrew_full: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר שָׁאוּל֩ אֶל־נֹשֵׂ֨א כֵלָ֜יו שְׁלֹ֥ף חַרְבְּךָ֣ וְדׇקְרֵ֣נִי בָ֗הּ פֶּן־יָבֹ֜אוּ הָעֲרֵלִ֤ים הָאֵ֙לֶּה֙ וְהִתְעַלְּלוּ־בִ֔י וְלֹ֤א אָבָה֙ נֹשֵׂ֣א כֵלָ֔יו כִּ֥י יָרֵ֖א מְאֹ֑דוַיִּקַּ֤ח שָׁאוּל֙ אֶת־הַחֶ֔רֶב וַיִּפֹּ֖ל עָלֶֽיהָ
verses.translation: Saul said to his arms-bearer, “Draw your sword and run me through, so that these uncircumcised may not come and make sport of me.” But his arms-bearer, out of great awe, refused; whereupon Saul grasped the sword and fell upon it.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·sword

verses.hebrew_full: וַיַּ֥רְא נֹשֵֽׂא־כֵלָ֖יו כִּ֣י מֵ֣ת שָׁא֑וּל וַיִּפֹּ֥ל גַּם־ה֛וּא עַל־הַחֶ֖רֶב וַיָּמֹֽת
verses.translation: When the arms-bearer saw that Saul was dead, he too fell on his sword and died.

verses.hebrew_full: וַיָּ֤מׇת שָׁאוּל֙ וּשְׁלֹ֣שֶׁת בָּנָ֔יו וְכׇל־בֵּית֖וֹ יַחְדָּ֥ו מֵֽתוּ
verses.translation: Thus Saul and his three sons and his entire house died together.

verses.hebrew_full: וַ֠יִּרְא֠וּ כׇּל־אִ֨ישׁ יִשְׂרָאֵ֤ל אֲשֶׁר־בָּעֵ֙מֶק֙ כִּ֣י נָ֔סוּ וְכִי־מֵ֖תוּ שָׁא֣וּל וּבָנָ֑יו וַיַּעַזְב֤וּ עָֽרֵיהֶם֙ וַיָּנֻ֔סוּ וַיָּבֹ֣אוּ פְלִשְׁתִּ֔ים וַיֵּשְׁב֖וּ בָּהֶֽם
verses.translation: And when everyone on Israel’s side in the valley saw that their army had fled and that Saul and his sons were dead, they abandoned their towns and fled; the Philistines then came and occupied them.

verses.hebrew_full: וַֽיְהִי֙ מִֽמׇּחֳרָ֔ת וַיָּבֹ֣אוּ פְלִשְׁתִּ֔ים לְפַשֵּׁ֖ט אֶת־הַחֲלָלִ֑ים וַֽיִּמְצְא֤וּ אֶת־שָׁאוּל֙ וְאֶת־בָּנָ֔יו נֹפְלִ֖ים בְּהַ֥ר גִּלְבֹּֽעַ
verses.translation: The next day the Philistines came to strip the slain, and they found Saul and his sons lying on Mount Gilboa.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·n m
sign of the definite direct object·subst
sign of the definite direct object·son

verses.hebrew_full: וַיַּ֨פְשִׁיטֻ֔הוּ וַיִּשְׂא֥וּ אֶת־רֹאשׁ֖וֹ וְאֶת־כֵּלָ֑יו וַיְשַׁלְּח֨וּ בְאֶרֶץ־פְּלִשְׁתִּ֜ים סָבִ֗יב לְבַשֵּׂ֛ר אֶת־עֲצַבֵּיהֶ֖ם וְאֶת־הָעָֽם
verses.translation: They stripped him, and carried off his head and his armor, and sent them throughout the land of the Philistines to spread the news to their idols and among the people.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·head
sign of the definite direct object·article
sign of the definite direct object·idol

verses.hebrew_full: וַיָּשִׂ֙ימוּ֙ אֶת־כֵּלָ֔יו בֵּ֖ית אֱלֹהֵיהֶ֑ם וְאֶת־גֻּלְגׇּלְתּ֥וֹ תָקְע֖וּ בֵּ֥ית דָּגֽוֹן
verses.translation: They placed his armor in the temple of their god, and they impaled his head in the temple of Dagan.

verses.hebrew_full: וַֽיִּשְׁמְע֔וּ כֹּ֖ל יָבֵ֣ישׁ גִּלְעָ֑ד אֵ֛ת כׇּל־אֲשֶׁר־עָשׂ֥וּ פְלִשְׁתִּ֖ים לְשָׁאֽוּל
verses.translation: When all Jabesh-gilead heard everything that the Philistines had done to Saul,

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object

verses.hebrew_full: וַיָּק֘וּמוּ֮ כׇּל־אִ֣ישׁ חַ֒יִל֒ וַיִּשְׂא֞וּ אֶת־גּוּפַ֣ת שָׁא֗וּל וְאֵת֙ גּוּפֹ֣ת בָּנָ֔יו וַיְבִיא֖וּם יָבֵ֑ישָׁה וַיִּקְבְּר֨וּ אֶת־עַצְמוֹתֵיהֶ֜ם תַּ֤חַת הָֽאֵלָה֙ בְּיָבֵ֔שׁ וַיָּצ֖וּמוּ שִׁבְעַ֥ת יָמִֽים
verses.translation: the stalwarts among them set out, removed the bodies of Saul and his sons, and brought them to Jabesh. They buried the bones under the oak tree in Jabesh, and they fasted for seven days.
verses.rashi: and brought them to Jabesh to Jabesh Gilead and they fasted seven days Why did they imperil their lives more than the rest of Israel to remove the bodies of Saul and his sons from Mount Gilboa? Because Saul had done them a favor by saving them when Nahash the Ammonite marched upon them. [They fasted seven days] corresponding to the seven days that they [the Ammonites] gave them [to see] if a savior would come [to save] them, [at which time] they fasted seven days; perhaps a savior would come to them. Since Saul came and saved them, they fasted now too seven days, and also because of the mourning which was customary to observe seven days, because they were also their kinsmen, for when Israel slew the inhabitants of Gibeah in the incident of the concubine in Gibeah (at the end of Judges), the people of Jabesh Gilead gave their daughters to Benjamin, when they grabbed them from the dances.

verses.hebrew_full: וַיָּ֣מׇת שָׁא֗וּל בְּמַֽעֲלוֹ֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר מָעַ֣ל בַּיהֹוָ֔ה עַל־דְּבַ֥ר יְהֹוָ֖ה אֲשֶׁ֣ר לֹֽא־שָׁמָ֑ר וְגַם־לִשְׁא֥וֹל בָּא֖וֹב לִדְרֽוֹשׁ
verses.translation: Saul died for the trespass that he had committed against GOD in not having fulfilled GOD’s command. Moreover, he had consulted a ghost to seek advice,
verses.rashi: And Saul died because of the treachery that he had committed He committed two treacherous acts by inquiring of the familiar spirit: one when he inquired after Samuel, and one when he inquired of familiar spirits, and in the Aggadah of Samuel (24:6) we learn that he committed five treacherous acts concerning the word of the Lord that he did not keep that which Samuel had commanded him, as it is written (I Sam. 10:8): “Seven days shall you wait,” and because he dealt treacherously in the war of Amalek [by sparing Agag].

verses.hebrew_full: וְלֹא־דָרַ֥שׁ בַּיהֹוָ֖ה וַיְמִיתֵ֑הוּ וַיַּסֵּב֙ אֶת־הַמְּלוּכָ֔ה לְדָוִ֖יד בֶּן־יִשָֽׁי
verses.translation: and did not seek advice of GOD, who had him slain—and had the kingdom transferred to David son of Jesse.

verses.hebrew_full: וַיִּקָּבְצ֧וּ כׇֽל־יִשְׂרָאֵ֛ל אֶל־דָּוִ֖יד חֶבְר֣וֹנָה לֵאמֹ֑ר הִנֵּ֛ה עַצְמְךָ֥ וּֽבְשָׂרְךָ֖ אֲנָֽחְנוּ
verses.translation: All Israel gathered to David at Hebron and said, “We are your own flesh and blood.

words.eng: we (first pers. pl. -usually used for emphasis)

verses.hebrew_full: גַּם־תְּמ֣וֹל גַּם־שִׁלְשׁ֗וֹם גַּ֚ם בִּֽהְי֣וֹת שָׁא֣וּל מֶ֔לֶךְ אַתָּ֛ה הַמּוֹצִ֥יא וְהַמֵּבִ֖יא אֶת־יִשְׂרָאֵ֑ל וַיֹּ֩אמֶר֩ יְהֹוָ֨ה אֱלֹהֶ֜יךָ לְךָ֗ אַתָּ֨ה תִרְעֶ֤ה אֶת־עַמִּי֙ אֶת־יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל וְאַתָּה֙ תִּֽהְיֶ֣ה נָגִ֔יד עַ֖ל עַמִּ֥י יִשְׂרָאֵֽל
verses.translation: Long before now, even when Saul was king, you were the leader of Israel; and the ETERNAL your God said to you: You shall shepherd My people Israel; you shall be ruler of My people Israel.”
verses.rashi: You shall shepherd My people Concerning this, David said, (Ps. 23:1): “The Lord is my Shepherd, I shall not want.” “You say to me: You shall shepherd. How can I shepherd? The matter does not depend on me, but the Lord is my Shepherd, I shall not want, and I, too, shall not be wanting to you.” A similar instance is found in Samuel (II 21:17): “Then the men of David swore to him, saying, ‘You shall no longer go out with us in battle, so that you extinguish not the lamp of Israel.’” David replied (ibid. 22:29): “For You are my lamp, O Lord; and the Lord does light my darkness.”

verses.hebrew_full: וַ֠יָּבֹ֠אוּ כׇּל־זִקְנֵ֨י יִשְׂרָאֵ֤ל אֶל־הַמֶּ֙לֶךְ֙ חֶבְר֔וֹנָה וַיִּכְרֹת֩ לָהֶ֨ם דָּוִ֥יד בְּרִ֛ית בְּחֶבְר֖וֹן לִפְנֵ֣י יְהֹוָ֑ה וַיִּמְשְׁח֨וּ אֶת־דָּוִ֤יד לְמֶ֙לֶךְ֙ עַל־יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל כִּדְבַ֥ר יְהֹוָ֖ה בְּיַד־שְׁמוּאֵֽל
verses.translation: All the elders of Israel came to the king at Hebron, and David made a pact with them in Hebron before GOD. And they anointed David king over Israel, according to the word of GOD through Samuel.
verses.rashi: and David made with them a covenant Like the covenant that Jehoiada made, as it is written (II Kings 11:17): “And Jehoiada enacted the covenant between the Lord and between the king and between the people, to be the people of the Lord.” The meaning is [that they agreed] to be servants of the Lord, and also “between the king and between the people,” to be his servants according to the king’s judgment, and also that the king should do according to the law for his servants; to wage their wars. before the Lord But is it not so that the Tabernacle was not in Hebron? What then is the meaning of “before the Lord”? Wherever people make a stipulation or enact a covenant, the Omnipresent is there. A similar instance [is found] in [the case of] Jephthah: (Jud. 11:11): “before the Lord in Mizpah.” [Another] similar instance (Lev. 5:21): “and commits an act of treachery against the Lord by making a denial to his neighbor,” as is explained in Torath Kohanim (ad loc.).

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·David = "beloved"

verses.hebrew_full: וַיֵּ֨לֶךְ דָּוִ֧יד וְכׇל־יִשְׂרָאֵ֛ל יְרוּשָׁלִַ֖ם הִ֣יא יְב֑וּס וְשָׁם֙ הַיְבוּסִ֔י יֹשְׁבֵ֖י הָאָֽרֶץ
verses.translation: David and all Israel set out for Jerusalem, that is Jebus, where the Jebusite inhabitants of the land lived.
verses.rashi: And David... went, etc. He went immediately to war when all Israel crowned him, so that Israel should not say [that in] all the wars that David waged in the days of Saul and was victorious it was Saul’s luck, and that he was now afraid to fight. Therefore he immediately went to war.

verses.hebrew_full: וַיֹּ֨אמְר֜וּ יֹשְׁבֵ֤י יְבוּס֙ לְדָוִ֔יד לֹ֥א תָב֖וֹא הֵ֑נָּה וַיִּלְכֹּ֤ד דָּוִיד֙ אֶת־מְצֻדַ֣ת צִיּ֔וֹן הִ֖יא עִ֥יר דָּוִֽיד
verses.translation: David was told by the inhabitants of Jebus, “You will never get in here!” But David captured the stronghold of Zion; it is now the City of David.
verses.rashi: And the inhabitants of Jebus said to David, “You shall not come here” He does not explain here why, but in Samuel (II 5:6), Scripture explains: “... and they said to David, saying: You shall not come here unless you remove the blind and the lame.” They saw that they were unable to withstand David. They therefore stationed blind and lame persons before the city gate, “as if to say: David shall not come here,” unless he wages war with these blind people, for they knew that it is disgraceful for a king to wage war against blind men, and because of this, he would turn back and not wage war with them. David said to himself, however, “It is true that it is neither right nor proper for me to wage war with the blind and with the lame, but ‘whoever smites the Jebusites’ in a way that he ‘reaches the tower’ shall pull them and cast them away from there, viz. the blind.” “Therefore they say: The blind and the lame shall not come into the house;” into the house of David, because it will be a disgrace for him, lest they say that David waged war with these [handicapped people].

verses.hebrew_full: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר דָּוִ֔יד כׇּל־מַכֵּ֤ה יְבוּסִי֙ בָּרִ֣אשׁוֹנָ֔ה יִהְיֶ֥ה לְרֹ֖אשׁ וּלְשָׂ֑ר וַיַּ֧עַל בָּרִאשׁוֹנָ֛ה יוֹאָ֥ב בֶּן־צְרוּיָ֖ה וַיְהִ֥י לְרֹֽאשׁ
verses.translation: David said, “Whoever attacks the Jebusites first will be the chief officer”; Joab son of Zeruiah attacked first, and became the chief.

verses.hebrew_full: וַיֵּ֥שֶׁב דָּוִ֖יד בַּמְצָ֑ד עַל־כֵּ֥ן קָרְאוּ־ל֖וֹ עִ֥יר דָּוִֽיד
verses.translation: David occupied the stronghold; therefore it was renamed the City of David.

verses.hebrew_full: וַיִּ֤בֶן הָעִיר֙ מִסָּבִ֔יב מִן־הַמִּלּ֖וֹא וְעַד־הַסָּבִ֑יב וְיוֹאָ֕ב יְחַיֶּ֖ה אֶת־שְׁאָ֥ר הָעִֽיר
verses.translation: David also fortified the surrounding area, from the Millo round about, and Joab rebuilt the rest of the city.
verses.rashi: and Joab restored the rest of the city Heb. יְחַיֶּה. He built and strengthened [it] by building [i.e., repairing] the cracks in the wall. A similar instance [is found] with Ezra (Neh. 3:34): “Will they revive (הַיְּחַיּוּ) the stones from the heaps of dust?”

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·leaven

verses.hebrew_full: וַיֵּ֥לֶךְ דָּוִ֖יד הָל֣וֹךְ וְגָד֑וֹל וַיהֹוָ֥ה צְבָא֖וֹת עִמּֽוֹ
verses.translation: David kept growing stronger, for GOD of Hosts was with him.

verses.hebrew_full: וְאֵ֨לֶּה רָאשֵׁ֤י הַגִּבֹּרִים֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר לְדָוִ֔יד הַמִּתְחַזְּקִ֨ים עִמּ֧וֹ בְמַלְכוּת֛וֹ עִם־כׇּל־יִשְׂרָאֵ֖ל לְהַמְלִיכ֑וֹ כִּדְבַ֥ר יְהֹוָ֖ה עַל־יִשְׂרָאֵֽל
verses.translation: And these were David’s chief warriors who strongly supported him in his kingdom, together with all Israel, to make him king, according to the word of GOD concerning Israel.

words.eng: to be or become king or queen

verses.hebrew_full: וְאֵ֛לֶּה מִסְפַּ֥ר הַגִּבֹּרִ֖ים אֲשֶׁ֣ר לְדָוִ֑יד יָֽשׇׁבְעָ֣ם בֶּן־חַכְמוֹנִ֗י רֹ֚אשׁ (השלושים) הַשָּׁ֣לִישִׁ֔ים הֽוּא־עוֹרֵ֧ר אֶת־חֲנִית֛וֹ עַל־שְׁלֹשׁ־מֵא֥וֹת חָלָ֖ל בְּפַ֥עַם אֶחָֽת
verses.translation: This is the list of David’s warriors: Jashobeam son of Hachmoni, the chief officer; he wielded his spear against three hundred and slew them all on one occasion.
verses.rashi: the head of the mighty men רֹאשׁ הַשָּׁלִישִׁים like (Exod. 14:7): “and mighty men וְשָׁלִשִׁים over them all.” he stirred his spear When he was engaged in battle, he would not return from the battle until he had slain three hundred men with his spear. the son of Hachmoni In Samuel (II 23:8), it is written: “Tahkemoni.” He is his father, and the father was stronger than the son, for it is written concerning him: “against eight hundred slain at one time.”

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·spear

verses.hebrew_full: וְאַחֲרָ֛יו אֶלְעָזָ֥ר בֶּן־דּוֹד֖וֹ הָאֲחוֹחִ֑י ה֖וּא בִּשְׁלוֹשָׁ֥ה הַגִּבֹּרִֽים
verses.translation: Next to him was Eleazar son of Dodo, the Ahohite; he was one of the three warriors.
verses.rashi: he was among the three mighty men He was included in the three mighty men; he was one of them. And these three were mightier than the rest of the thirty three mentioned nearby. Here he counts only two: Jashobeam and Eleazar, but in Samuel (II 23:11), it mentions the third one as well, as it is written: “and after him [came] Shammah the son of Agei the mountaineer”

verses.hebrew_full: הֽוּא־הָיָ֨ה עִם־דָּוִ֜יד בַּפַּ֣ס דַּמִּ֗ים וְהַפְּלִשְׁתִּים֙ נֶאֶסְפוּ־שָׁ֣ם לַמִּלְחָמָ֔ה וַתְּהִ֛י חֶלְקַ֥ת הַשָּׂדֶ֖ה מְלֵאָ֣ה שְׂעוֹרִ֑ים וְהָעָ֥ם נָ֖סוּ מִפְּנֵ֥י פְלִשְׁתִּֽים
verses.translation: He was with David at Pas Dammim when the Philistines gathered there for battle. There was a plot of ground full of barley there; the troops had fled from the Philistines,
verses.rashi: in Pas-Dammim a name of a place, and in Samuel (I 17:1): “in Ephes-Dammim.” and the Philistines gathered there for war they were eager to avenge themselves upon Israel and those mighty men who reviled them, as delineated in Samuel (II 23:9): “...when they reviled the Philistines.” full of barley and the Philistines wished to reap it and to set it afire.

verses.hebrew_full: וַיִּֽתְיַצְּב֤וּ בְתוֹךְ־הַֽחֶלְקָה֙ וַיַּצִּיל֔וּהָ וַיַּכּ֖וּ אֶת־פְּלִשְׁתִּ֑ים וַיּ֥וֹשַׁע יְהֹוָ֖ה תְּשׁוּעָ֥ה גְדוֹלָֽה
verses.translation: but they took their stand in the middle of the plot and defended it, and they routed the Philistines. Thus GOD wrought a great victory.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·Philistine = "immigrants"

verses.hebrew_full: וַיֵּרְד֡וּ שְֽׁלוֹשָׁה֩ מִן־הַשְּׁלוֹשִׁ֨ים רֹ֤אשׁ עַל־הַצֻּר֙ אֶל־דָּוִ֔יד אֶל־מְעָרַ֖ת עֲדֻלָּ֑ם וּמַחֲנֵ֣ה פְלִשְׁתִּ֔ים חֹנָ֖ה בְּעֵ֥מֶק רְפָאִֽים
verses.translation: Three of the thirty chiefs went down to the rock to David, at the cave of Adullam, while a force of Philistines was encamped in the Valley of Rephaim.
verses.rashi: And three of the thirty These three were the heads of the entire thirty.

verses.hebrew_full: וְדָוִ֖יד אָ֣ז בַּמְּצוּדָ֑ה וּנְצִ֣יב פְּלִשְׁתִּ֔ים אָ֖ז בְּבֵ֥ית לָֽחֶם
verses.translation: David was then in the stronghold, and a Philistine garrison was then at Bethlehem.
verses.rashi: and a Philistine governor was then in Bethlehem for they had already taken Bethlehem and stationed a governor therein, who judged [the region]. This was at the beginning of his reign, for the Philistines were still ruling over them, for so we find that prior to David’s arrival, they ruled over Israel in the days of Samson and Samuel, as it is written (I Samuel 4:9): “Strengthen yourselves and be valiant, Philistines, lest you serve the Hebrews, etc.”

verses.hebrew_full: וַיִּתְאָ֥ו דָּוִ֖יד וַיֹּאמַ֑ר מִ֚י יַשְׁקֵ֣נִי מַ֔יִם מִבּ֥וֹר בֵּֽית־לֶ֖חֶם אֲשֶׁ֥ר בַּשָּֽׁעַר
verses.translation: David felt a craving and said, “If only I could get a drink of water from the cistern that is by the gate of Bethlehem!”
verses.rashi: If only someone would give me to drink water from the cistern of Bethlehem This was David’s place, as it is written (I Sam. 17:58): “The son of your servant, Jesse the Bethlehemite,” and because he was accustomed and used to that water, he desired it; any water or air that a person is accustomed to is beneficial to him, and that which he is not used to is harmful to him. A similar case is [found] in the beginning of Josiphon about a queen who became ill because she was carried away from the land of her birth, and all the physicians said, “She has no cure until she bathes and derives benefit from the water of her place,” and they did this for her.

verses.hebrew_full: וַיִּבְקְע֨וּ הַשְּׁלֹשָׁ֜ה בְּמַחֲנֵ֣ה פְלִשְׁתִּ֗ים וַיִּֽשְׁאֲבוּ־מַ֙יִם֙ מִבּ֤וֹר בֵּֽית־לֶ֙חֶם֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר בַּשַּׁ֔עַר וַיִּשְׂא֖וּ וַיָּבִ֣אוּ אֶל־דָּוִ֑יד וְלֹא־אָבָ֤ה דָוִיד֙ לִשְׁתּוֹתָ֔ם וַיְנַסֵּ֥ךְ אֹתָ֖ם לַיהֹוָֽה
verses.translation: So the three got through the Philistine camp, and drew water from the cistern that is by the gate of Bethlehem, and they carried it back to David. But David would not drink it, and he poured it out as a libation to GOD.
verses.rashi: And the three broke through the Philistine camp (In German, durchbrechen die schare) drew When the Philistines were standing before the cistern, the three mighty men of Israel broke through between them and drew [water] against their will. and he poured it out as a libation to the Lord Bar Kappara says: It was the festival of Succoth, and he poured them [the waters] out for a libation on the altar.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object

verses.hebrew_full: וַיֹּ֡אמֶר חָלִ֩ילָה֩ לִּ֨י מֵאֱלֹהַ֜י מֵעֲשׂ֣וֹת זֹ֗את הֲדַ֣ם הָאֲנָשִׁים֩ הָאֵ֨לֶּה אֶשְׁתֶּ֤ה בְנַפְשׁוֹתָם֙ כִּ֣י בְנַפְשׁוֹתָ֣ם הֱבִיא֔וּם וְלֹ֥א אָבָ֖ה לִשְׁתּוֹתָ֑ם אֵ֣לֶּה עָשׂ֔וּ שְׁלֹ֖שֶׁת הַגִּבּוֹרִֽים
verses.translation: For he said, “God forbid that I should do this! Can I drink the blood of these men who risked their lives?”—for they had brought it at the risk of their lives, and he would not drink it. Such were the exploits of the three warriors.
verses.rashi: for they brought it with their lives for they risked being killed for the water.

verses.hebrew_full: וְאַבְשַׁ֣י אֲחִֽי־יוֹאָ֗ב ה֤וּא הָיָה֙ רֹ֣אשׁ הַשְּׁלוֹשָׁ֔ה וְהוּא֙ עוֹרֵ֣ר אֶת־חֲנִית֔וֹ עַל־שְׁלֹ֥שׁ מֵא֖וֹת חָלָ֑ל (ולא) וְלוֹ־שֵׁ֖ם בַּשְּׁלוֹשָֽׁה
verses.translation: Abshai, the brother of Joab, was head of another three. He once wielded his spear against three hundred and slew them. He won a name among the three;
verses.rashi: And Abshai, the brother of Joab, was the chief of the three of those three who broke through the Philistine camp and drew the water. The reason he does not mention Joab with the chiefs of the mighty men is that it is beneath his dignity to count him with these men, because he was the general of the army, the chief of them all. and he had a name among the three It was reputed (lit., he had that name) that his heroism equaled all the heroic acts of them all, but it was an error.

words.eng: Abishai = "my father is Jesse" or "my father is a gift"
sign of the definite direct object·spear

verses.hebrew_full: מִן־הַשְּׁלוֹשָׁ֤ה בַשְּׁנַ֙יִם֙ נִכְבָּ֔ד וַיְהִ֥י לָהֶ֖ם לְשָׂ֑ר וְעַד־הַשְּׁלוֹשָׁ֖ה לֹא־בָֽא
verses.translation: among the three he was more highly regarded than the other two, and so he became their commander. However, he did not attain to the other three.
verses.rashi: Of the three, he was honored like two He was not honored like those three. His heroism equalled that of the two, but he did not attain the amount of heroism of the three, and that is the meaning of “but to the three he did not come.”

verses.hebrew_full: בְּנָיָ֨ה בֶן־יְהוֹיָדָ֧ע בֶּן־אִֽישׁ־חַ֛יִל רַב־פְּעָלִ֖ים מִֽן־קַבְצְאֵ֑ל ה֣וּא הִכָּ֗ה אֵ֣ת שְׁנֵ֤י אֲרִיאֵל֙ מוֹאָ֔ב וְ֠ה֠וּא יָרַ֞ד וְהִכָּ֧ה אֶֽת־הָאֲרִ֛י בְּת֥וֹךְ הַבּ֖וֹר בְּי֥וֹם הַשָּֽׁלֶג
verses.translation: Benaiah son of Jehoiada from Kabzeel was a brave soldier who performed great deeds. He killed the two [sons] of Ariel of Moab. Once, on a snowy day, he went down into a pit and killed a lion.
verses.rashi: a valiant man בֶּן אִישׁ חַַיִל, similar to בֶּן בְּלִיַּעַל, an unscrupulous man, and in Samuel (II 23:20) it is written: בֶּן אִישׁ חַי for it is customary for people, when they see an agile person, to say, “This one is full of life.” mighty men אֲרִיאֵל mighty men, possessing strength of a lion, a mighty man, and [it] is also an expression of (Ezek. 17: 20): “...and he took the mighty (אֵילֵי) of the land.” who accomplished many feats He accomplished many feats of heroism. and he descended and smote the lion in the midst of a pit on a snowy day and he did not fear the cold, and some say [that the heroism] of the snowy day is that throughout the whole year the lion is not as dangerous as on a snowy day. When a person comes against him, he throws the snow with his feet between the person’s eyes until he is unable to see, and he kills him. I heard this [meaning] of this [verse].

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object
sign of the definite direct object·lion

verses.hebrew_full: וְהֽוּא־הִכָּה֩ אֶת־הָאִ֨ישׁ הַמִּצְרִ֜י אִ֥ישׁ מִדָּ֣ה חָמֵ֣שׁ בָּאַמָּ֗ה וּבְיַ֨ד הַמִּצְרִ֤י חֲנִית֙ כִּמְנ֣וֹר אֹֽרְגִ֔ים וַיֵּ֥רֶד אֵלָ֖יו בַּשָּׁ֑בֶט וַיִּגְזֹ֤ל אֶֽת־הַחֲנִית֙ מִיַּ֣ד הַמִּצְרִ֔י וַיַּהַרְגֵ֖הוּ בַּחֲנִיתֽוֹ
verses.translation: He also killed an Egyptian, a giant of a man five cubits tall. The Egyptian had a spear in his hand, like a weaver’s beam, yet [Benaiah] went down against him with a club, wrenched the spear out of the Egyptian’s hand, and killed him with his own spear.
verses.rashi: a man of great stature Other people do not require to be measured, as [their height] can be determined by estimate; a similar case is (Num. 13:32): “... whom we saw in its midst are men of a measure.” like a weavers’ beam the pole upon which the cloth is wrapped.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·man
sign of the definite direct object·spear

verses.hebrew_full: אֵ֣לֶּה עָשָׂ֔ה בְּנָיָ֖הוּ בֶּן־יְהוֹיָדָ֑ע וְלוֹ־שֵׁ֖ם בִּשְׁלוֹשָׁ֥ה הַגִּבֹּרִֽים
verses.translation: Such were the exploits of Benaiah son of Jehoiada; and he won a name among the three warriors.

words.eng: Benaiah = "Jehovah has built" or "Yahweh has built up"

verses.hebrew_full: מִן־הַשְּׁלוֹשִׁ֗ים הִנּ֤וֹ נִכְבָּד֙ ה֔וּא וְאֶל־הַשְּׁלֹשָׁ֖ה לֹא־בָ֑א וַיְשִׂימֵ֥הוּ דָוִ֖יד עַל־מִשְׁמַעְתּֽוֹ
verses.translation: He was highly regarded among the thirty, but he did not attain to the three. David put him in charge of his bodyguard.
verses.rashi: Of the thirty, he was the most honored, but to the three he did not come To the heroic feats of the three he did not reach; i.e., to those three mentioned above (verse 20). Therefore, לא is written above with an “aleph.” over his guard עַל מִשְׁמַעְתּוֹ, an expression of appointment, and likewise, (Isa. 11:14): “... and the children of Ammon will be their appointees מִשְׁמַעְתָּם.”

verses.hebrew_full: וְגִבּוֹרֵ֖י הַחֲיָלִ֑ים עֲשָׂהאֵל֙ אֲחִ֣י יוֹאָ֔באֶלְחָנָ֥ן בֶּן־דּוֹד֖וֹ מִבֵּ֥ית לָֽחֶם
verses.translation: The valiant warriors: Asahel brother of Joab, Elhanan son of Dodo from Bethlehem,
verses.rashi: And the mighty warriors but not like those above.

verses.hebrew_full: שַׁמּוֹת֙ הַהֲרוֹרִ֔יחֶ֖לֶץ הַפְּלוֹנִֽי
verses.translation: Shammoth the Harorite, Helez the Pelonite,

verses.hebrew_full: עִירָ֤א בֶן־עִקֵּשׁ֙ הַתְּקוֹעִ֔יאֲבִיעֶ֖זֶר הָעַנְּתוֹתִֽי
verses.translation: Ira son of Ikkesh from Tekoa, Abiezer of Anathoth,

verses.hebrew_full: סִבְּכַי֙ הַחֻ֣שָׁתִ֔יעִילַ֖י הָאֲחוֹחִֽי
verses.translation: Sibbecai the Hushathite, Ilai the Ahohite,

verses.hebrew_full: מַהְרַי֙ הַנְּטֹ֣פָתִ֔יחֵ֥לֶד בֶּֽן־בַּעֲנָ֖ה הַנְּטוֹפָתִֽי
verses.translation: Mahrai the Netophathite, Heled son of Baanah the Netophathite,

verses.hebrew_full: אִיתַ֣י בֶּן־רִיבַ֗י מִגִּבְעַת֙ בְּנֵ֣י בִנְיָמִ֔ןבְּנָיָ֖ה הַפִּרְעָתֹנִֽי
verses.translation: Ittai son of Ribai from Gibeah of the Benjaminites, Benaiah of Pirathon,

verses.hebrew_full: חוּרַי֙ מִנַּ֣חֲלֵי גָ֔עַשׁאֲבִיאֵ֖ל הָעַרְבָתִֽי
verses.translation: Hurai of Nahale-gaash, Abiel the Arbathite,

verses.hebrew_full: עַזְמָ֙וֶת֙ הַבַּ֣חֲרוּמִ֔יאֶלְיַחְבָּ֖א הַשַּׁעַלְבֹנִֽי
verses.translation: Azmaveth the Bahrumite, Eliahba of Shaalbon,

verses.hebrew_full: בְּנֵ֗י הָשֵׁם֙ הַגִּ֣זוֹנִ֔ייוֹנָתָ֥ן בֶּן־שָׁגֵ֖ה הַהֲרָרִֽי
verses.translation: the sons of Hashem the Gizonite, Jonathan son of Shageh the Hararite,

verses.hebrew_full: אֲחִיאָ֧ם בֶּן־שָׂכָ֛ר הַהֲרָרִ֖יאֱלִיפַ֥ל בֶּן־אֽוּר
verses.translation: Ahiam son of Sacar the Hararite, Eliphal son of Ur,

verses.hebrew_full: חֵ֚פֶר הַמְּכֵ֣רָתִ֔י אֲחִיָּ֖ה הַפְּלֹנִֽי
verses.translation: Hepher the Mecherathite, Ahijah the Pelonite,

words.eng: Mecherathite = "he of the dug-out" or "he of the digging tool"

verses.hebrew_full: חֶצְרוֹ֙ הַֽכַּרְמְלִ֔ינַעֲרַ֖י בֶּן־אֶזְבָּֽי
verses.translation: Hezro the Carmelite, Naarai son of Ezbai,

verses.hebrew_full: יוֹאֵל֙ אֲחִ֣י נָתָ֔ןמִבְחָ֖ר בֶּן־הַגְרִֽי
verses.translation: Joel brother of Nathan, Mibhar son of Hagri,

verses.hebrew_full: צֶ֖לֶק הָעַמּוֹנִ֑י נַחְרַי֙ הַבֵּ֣רֹתִ֔י נֹשֵׂ֕א כְּלֵ֖י יוֹאָ֥ב בֶּן־צְרוּיָֽה
verses.translation: Zelek the Ammonite, Naharai the Berothite—the arms-bearer of Joab son of Zeruiah—

verses.hebrew_full: עִירָא֙ הַיִּתְרִ֔יגָּרֵ֖ב הַיִּתְרִֽי
verses.translation: Ira the Ithrite, Gareb the Ithrite,

verses.hebrew_full: אֽוּרִיָּה֙ הַחִתִּ֔יזָבָ֖ד בֶּן־אַחְלָֽי
verses.translation: Uriah the Hittite, Zabad son of Ahlai.

words.eng: between·Oh that...!; oh would that!; ah that!

verses.hebrew_full: עֲדִינָ֨א בֶן־שִׁיזָ֜א הָראוּבֵנִ֗י רֹ֛אשׁ לָראוּבֵנִ֖י וְעָלָ֥יו שְׁלֹשִֽׁים
verses.translation: Adina son of Shiza the Reubenite, a chief of the Reubenites, and thirty with him;

verses.hebrew_full: חָנָן֙ בֶּֽן־מַעֲכָ֔ה וְיוֹשָׁפָ֖ט הַמִּתְנִֽי
verses.translation: Hanan son of Maacah, and Joshaphat the Mithnite;

words.eng: Elon Beth-hanan = "oak of the house of grace"
between·Abel Beth Maachah = "meadow of the house of Maachah"

verses.hebrew_full: עֻזִּיָּ֖א הָעַשְׁתְּרָתִ֑ישָׁמָע֙ (ויעואל) וִיעִיאֵ֔ל בְּנֵ֖י חוֹתָ֥ם הָעֲרֹעֵרִֽי
verses.translation: Uzziah the Ashterathite, Shama and Jeiel sons of Hotham the Aroerite;

verses.hebrew_full: יְדִֽיעֲאֵל֙ בֶּן־שִׁמְרִ֔י וְיֹחָ֥א אָחִ֖יו הַתִּיצִֽי
verses.translation: Jedaiael son of Shimri, and Joha his brother, the Tizite;

verses.hebrew_full: אֱלִיאֵל֙ הַֽמַּחֲוִ֔ים וִירִיבַ֥י וְיוֹשַׁוְיָ֖ה בְּנֵ֣י אֶלְנָ֑עַםוְיִתְמָ֖ה הַמּוֹאָבִֽי
verses.translation: Eliel the Mahavite, and Jeribai and Joshaviah sons of Elnaam, and Ithmah the Moabite;

words.eng: Eliel = "my God is God" or "El is God"

verses.hebrew_full: אֱלִיאֵ֣ל וְעוֹבֵ֔דוְיַעֲשִׂיאֵ֖ל הַמְּצֹבָיָֽה
verses.translation: Eliel, Obed, and Jaassiel the Mezobaite.

words.eng: Eliel = "my God is God" or "El is God"
Mesobaite = "the one set up of JAH"

verses.hebrew_full: וְאֵ֗לֶּה הַבָּאִ֤ים אֶל־דָּוִיד֙ לְצִ֣יקְלַ֔ג ע֣וֹד עָצ֔וּר מִפְּנֵ֖י שָׁא֣וּל בֶּן־קִ֑ישׁ וְהֵ֙מָּה֙ בַּגִּבּוֹרִ֔ים עֹזְרֵ֖י הַמִּלְחָמָֽה
verses.translation: The following joined David at Ziklag while he was still in hiding from Saul son of Kish; these were the warriors who gave support in battle;
verses.rashi: when he was still hiding - Heb. עָצוּר, hiding.

verses.hebrew_full: נֹ֣שְׁקֵי קֶ֗שֶׁת מַיְמִינִ֤ים וּמַשְׂמִאלִים֙ בָּאֲבָנִ֔ים וּבַחִצִּ֖ים בַּקָּ֑שֶׁת מֵאֲחֵ֥י שָׁא֖וּל מִבִּנְיָמִֽן
verses.translation: they were armed with the bow and could use both right hand and left hand to sling stones or shoot arrows with the bow; they were kindred of Saul from Benjamin.
verses.rashi: Armed with bows armed with a bow and with arrows and shooting in front of them and behind them. And they would even place stones on the bowstring and shoot. I personally saw many like these. of Saul’s brethren of Benjamin Since they were all descended from one man, from Benjamin, they are all called brethren, and in honor of David’s greatness, the Chronicler says, “of Saul’s brethren,” [saying] that even Saul’s brethren came to him while Saul was still living.

verses.hebrew_full: הָרֹ֨אשׁ אֲחִיעֶ֜זֶר וְיוֹאָ֗שׁ בְּנֵי֙ הַשְּׁמָעָ֣ה הַגִּבְעָתִ֔י (ויזואל) וִיזִיאֵ֥ל וָפֶ֖לֶט בְּנֵ֣י עַזְמָ֑וֶת וּבְרָכָ֕ה וְיֵה֖וּא הָעַנְּתֹתִֽי
verses.translation: At the head were Ahiezer and Joash, sons of Shemaah of Gibeah; and Jeziel and Pelet, sons of Azmaveth; and Beracah and Jehu of Anathoth;

verses.hebrew_full: וְיִֽשְׁמַעְיָ֧ה הַגִּבְעוֹנִ֛י גִּבּ֥וֹר בַּשְּׁלֹשִׁ֖ים וְעַל־הַשְּׁלֹשִֽׁים
verses.translation: Ishmaiah of Gibeon, a warrior among the thirty, leading the thirty;
verses.rashi: and over the thirty the chief of the thirty.

verses.hebrew_full: וְיִרְמְיָ֤ה וְיַחֲזִיאֵל֙ וְי֣וֹחָנָ֔ן וְיוֹזָבָ֖ד הַגְּדֵרָתִֽי
verses.translation: Jeremiah, Jahaziel, Johanan, and Jozabad of Gederah;

verses.hebrew_full: אֶלְעוּזַ֤י וִֽירִימוֹת֙ וּבְעַלְיָ֣ה וּשְׁמַרְיָ֔הוּ וּשְׁפַטְיָ֖הוּ (החריפי) הַחֲרוּפִֽי
verses.translation: Eluzai, Jerimoth, Bealiah, Shemariah, and Shephatiah the Hariphite;

words.eng: Jerimoth or Jeremoth = "He is Most High"

verses.hebrew_full: אֶלְקָנָ֡ה וְ֠יִשִּׁיָּ֠הוּ וַעֲזַרְאֵ֧ל וְיוֹעֶ֛זֶר וְיָשׇׁבְעָ֖ם הַקׇּרְחִֽים
verses.translation: Elkanah, Isshiah, Azarel, Joezer, and Jashobeam the Korahites;

words.eng: Elkanah = "God has possessed" or "God has created"
Ishiah or Ishijah or Jesiah = "Jehovah will lend"

verses.hebrew_full: וְיוֹעֵאלָ֧ה וּזְבַדְיָ֛ה בְּנֵ֥י יְרֹחָ֖ם מִֽן־הַגְּדֽוֹר
verses.translation: Joelah and Zebadiah, sons of Jeroham of Gedor. with several mss.

verses.hebrew_full: וּמִן־הַגָּדִ֡י נִבְדְּל֣וּ אֶל־דָּוִיד֩ לַמְצַ֨ד מִדְבָּ֜רָה גִּבֹּרֵ֣י הַחַ֗יִל אַנְשֵׁ֤י צָבָא֙ לַמִּלְחָמָ֔ה עֹרְכֵ֥י צִנָּ֖ה וָרֹ֑מַח וּפְנֵ֤י אַרְיֵה֙ פְּנֵיהֶ֔ם וְכִצְבָאיִ֥ם עַל־הֶהָרִ֖ים לְמַהֵֽר
verses.translation: Of the Gadites, there withdrew to follow David to the wilderness stronghold valiant warriors, fighters fit for battle, armed with shield and spear; they had the appearance of lions, and were as swift as gazelles upon the mountains:
verses.rashi: to the stronghold of the desert to the strongholds of the desert where he hid from Saul, as it is written (I Sam. 23:24): “And they arose and went to Ziph before Saul, and David and his men were in the desert of Maon, in the plain,” and it is written (ibid. 24:1): “And David went up from there, and he stayed in the strongholds of En-Gedi.” and whose faces were [like] the faces of lions Their faces were frightful like lions. and as swift as gazelles on the mountains They were swift in their running, for it is customary for gazelles to run on the mountains when people hunt them.

verses.hebrew_full: עֵ֖זֶר הָרֹ֑אשׁ עֹֽבַדְיָה֙ הַשֵּׁנִ֔י אֱלִיאָ֖ב הַשְּׁלִשִֽׁי
verses.translation: Ezer the chief, Obadiah the second, Eliab the third,

words.eng: Eliab = "my God is father" or "God is father"

verses.hebrew_full: מִשְׁמַנָּה֙ הָרְבִיעִ֔י יִרְמְיָ֖ה הַחֲמִשִֽׁי
verses.translation: Mashmannah the fourth, Jeremiah the fifth,

verses.hebrew_full: עַתַּי֙ הַשִּׁשִּׁ֔י אֱלִיאֵ֖ל הַשְּׁבִעִֽי
verses.translation: Attai the sixth, Eliel the seventh,

words.eng: Eliel = "my God is God" or "El is God"

verses.hebrew_full: יֽוֹחָנָן֙ הַשְּׁמִינִ֔י אֶלְזָבָ֖ד הַתְּשִׁיעִֽי
verses.translation: Johanan the eighth, Elzabad the ninth,

verses.hebrew_full: יִרְמְיָ֙הוּ֙ הָעֲשִׂירִ֔י מַכְבַּנַּ֖י עַשְׁתֵּ֥י עָשָֽׂר
verses.translation: Jeremiah the tenth, Machbannai the eleventh.

verses.hebrew_full: אֵ֥לֶּה מִבְּנֵי־גָ֖ד רָאשֵׁ֣י הַצָּבָ֑א אֶחָ֤ד לְמֵאָה֙ הַקָּטָ֔ן וְהַגָּד֖וֹל לְאָֽלֶף
verses.translation: Those were the Gadites, heads of the army. The least was equal to a hundred, the greatest to a thousand.
verses.rashi: these were some of the sons of Gad as is written above (v. 8): “And some of the Gadites separated to David.” the smallest one a hundred The smallest of them could pursue a hundred. and the greatest a thousand And the greatest of them could pursue a thousand. This is an actualization of the verse (Lev. 26:8): “Five of you will pursue one hundred, and one hundred of you, etc.”

verses.hebrew_full: אֵ֣לֶּה הֵ֗ם אֲשֶׁ֨ר עָבְר֤וּ אֶת־הַיַּרְדֵּן֙ בַּחֹ֣דֶשׁ הָרִאשׁ֔וֹן וְה֥וּא מְמַלֵּ֖א עַל־כׇּל־גְּדוֹתָ֑יו (גדיתיו) וַיַּבְרִ֙יחוּ֙ אֶת־כׇּל־הָ֣עֲמָקִ֔ים לַמִּזְרָ֖ח וְלַֽמַּעֲרָֽב
verses.translation: These were the ones who crossed the Jordan in the first month, when it was at its crest, and they put to flight all the lowlanders to the east and west.
verses.rashi: These are the ones who crossed the Jordan in the first month in Nissan, when the snows had already melted. and they put all the valley-dwellers to flight to the east and to the west They crossed the Jordan and with their shields split all the depths of the water to the east and to the west and crossed on dry land. Some say that ויַּבְרִיחוּ אֶת כָּל הָעֲמָקִִים refers to the people dwelling in the valleys.

words.eng: to pass over or by or through
sign of the definite direct object·Jordan = "descender"
sign of the definite direct object·all·valley

verses.hebrew_full: וַיָּבֹ֗אוּ מִן־בְּנֵ֤י בִנְיָמִן֙ וִֽיהוּדָ֔ה עַד־לַמְצָ֖ד לְדָוִֽיד
verses.translation: Some of the Benjaminites and Judahites came to the stronghold to David,
verses.rashi: And there came some of the sons of Benjamin and Judah up to the stronghold, to David to the stronghold where David was hiding from Saul. And they took David’s sister’s son with them, because the sons of Benjamin were concerned that David might think that the sons of Benjamin had come to bind him and deliver him into Saul’s hands. Therefore, they took Amasa with them to David, and also some of David’s kinsmen from the rest of Judah, as it is written, “some of the sons of Benjamin and Judah,” in order that David would see that they came with them in good faith, so that he should not suspect them of anything.

verses.hebrew_full: וַיֵּצֵ֣א דָוִיד֮ לִפְנֵיהֶם֒ וַיַּ֙עַן֙ וַיֹּ֣אמֶר לָהֶ֔ם אִם־לְשָׁל֞וֹם בָּאתֶ֤ם אֵלַי֙ לְעׇזְרֵ֔נִי יִֽהְיֶה־לִּ֧י עֲלֵיכֶ֛ם לֵבָ֖ב לְיָ֑חַד וְאִֽם־לְרַמּוֹתַ֣נִי לְצָרַ֗י בְּלֹ֤א חָמָס֙ בְּכַפַּ֔י יֵ֛רֶא אֱלֹהֵ֥י אֲבוֹתֵ֖ינוּ וְיוֹכַֽח
verses.translation: and David went out to meet them, saying to them, “If you come on a peaceful errand, to support me, then I will make common cause with you, but if to betray me to my foes, for no injustice on my part, then let the God of our ancestors take notice and give judgment.”
verses.rashi: And David came out before them from the stronghold. my heart will be upon you as one My heart and your heart will be like one heart. but if you have come to betray me to my adversaries although there is no injustice in my hands for what have I done to Saul that he is stalking my soul to take it for nothing?

words.eng: to be·inflected pers. pron. meaning ‘to me’.
